question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to his statement response to AQO 1963/22-27, to detail whether he has had an opportunity to consider (i) the judgement in For Women Scotland Ltd v the Scottish Ministers; and (ii) its implications for Departmental policy.
answer_text: On May 12, I articulated to the Assembly my clear position that I do not believe that a boy who identifies as a girl should participate in girls' competitive sports or use girls' changing and toilet facilities.I also believe that neither pupils nor teachers should be compelled to use pronouns other than those which reflect biological sex.While many of these issues are best managed at a school level, consistent with the ethos of the school, I think it is important that the Department sets out some guiding principles.Given the complexity of some of the issues involved in policy, legal and administrative terms I asked officials to consider the implications of the recent Supreme Court decision and provide me with a secure legal basis on which to take decisions.Advice from officials including legal advice from Senior Counsel was received by me on 7 August. The central holding of the advice that I received was that consistent with the finding of the Supreme Court, and taking into account matters which are specific to Northern Ireland, in relevant Northern Ireland legislation, references to sex are references to biological sex. This has significant implications, in particular, for the Education Authority which in 2019 produced, “Guidance for Schools, EOTAS Centres and Youth Services on supporting Transgender Young People.”I have reflected on the policy I articulated in May, and on the advice that I have subsequently received, and have concluded that the present Education Authority guidance neither reflects Departmental policy nor is it consistent with the law in Northern Ireland.Consequently, I have written to the Education Authority to ask that the guidance is withdrawn immediately.I am conscious that these issues require to be handled sensitively and of the fact that the guidance was issued by the Education Authority and not the Department of Education, however policy in this area must reflect what is in the best interest of all children and not politically motivated activist groups which do not reflect the views of wider society.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detil any assessment his Department has made in relation to the potential risks associated with the volatility and geopolitical concerns surrounding refuse-derived fuel markets.
answer_text: Refuse derived fuel (RDF) is a waste with a calorific value that can be used as energy from waste. The aim of the Department is to move waste as far up the waste hierarchy as possible, but not all waste can be fully recycled, so energy recovery is the next best option and in most cases is preferable to landfilling.NI does not currently have the capacity to use all the RDF that is produced from recycling operations. It is therefore exported, currently under Regulation (EU) 1013/2006 on shipments of waste (and Regulation (EU) 2024/1157 from May 2026) to countries that have the facilities and capacity to use it.Companies who export RDF internationally operate under this Regulation and a Code of Practice which seeks to ensure that the industry delivers environmental benefits in alignment with the waste hierarchy, operates safely and adheres to relevant contractual agreements.Many elements of contingency planning sit outside the remit of DAERA and are largely market-led, however, my officials continue to collaborate and play their part in supporting the decision-making process.  The Department has commissioned the SIB to undertake an analysis of NI waste arisings and infrastructure through to 2050. The findings of this review should inform future capacity decisions including consideration of the feasibility of alternatives to waste exports.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding provided to Ulster Rugby in the last 12 months.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“In the last 12 months, April 2024 to March 2025, Sport NI awarded £452,500 to Irish Rugby Football Union’s Ulster Branch as per table one below.Table 1: IRFU Ulster Branch Award 2024/25Fiscal YearFundTypeProject TitleGrant Amount2024/25LotteryRevenueSports Systems Investment Governing Bodies 2024-25£450,0002024/25LotteryRevenueAthlete Awards component of Sports Systems Investment 2024-25£2,500“Please be advised that a further £455,000 has been awarded for the period April 2025 to March 2026 as per table two below.Table 2: IRFU Ulster Branch Award 2025/26Fiscal YearFundTypeProject TitleGrant Amount2025/26LotteryRevenueSports Systems Investment Governing Bodies 2025-26£450,0002025/26LotteryRevenueAthlete Awards component of Sports Systems Investment 2025-26 £5,000”In addition, my Department has provided funding of £64,072.05 in 2024/25 and £69,838.53 to Ulster Rugby from the Innovation fund through the Sport, a Home for Lifelong Volunteering programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of households in receipt of benefits in excess of £28,000.
answer_text: According to the Family Resources Survey in the 2023/24 financial year an estimated 35,000 households in Northern Ireland (NI) had income from benefits in excess of £28,000. This figure represents 5% of all households, or 7% of households in receipt of benefits in NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 28859/22-27, when the Coastal Forum last met.
answer_text: I can confirm that the Coastal Forum last met on 14th March 2022, and DfI officials were in attendance at this meeting.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any action his Department is taking in relation to working with industry in the production of Biochar and its potential application to assist with achieving carbon reduction targets.
answer_text: My Department is aware of the potential for biochar to contribute to net-zero targets and has funded a number of R&D projects on this topic over the past 5 years which will add to the expanding body of evidence supporting both industry innovation and policy formation.  There are a number of DAERA funded Small Business Research Initiatives supporting industry-led innovation in the sustainable use of agricultural biomass, though none which involve biochar production.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding that has been provided to (i) Northern Cricket Union; and (ii) North West Cricket Union, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The NCU and NWCU are two of the five provincial governing bodies for cricket in Ireland, along with Connacht, Leinster, Munster. These five unions collectively form Cricket Ireland. Sport NI has not funded Northern Cricket Union and North West Cricket Union directly. Sport NI made an award of £457,000 to Cricket Ireland in 2024/25, and a second award of £457,000 to Cricket Ireland for 2025/26. Please be advised that Northern Cricket Union and North West Cricket Union are affiliated to Cricket Ireland. Please note that all funding from Sport NI to all island NGB’s, including Cricket Ireland, require that funding be spent for the development of sport in Northern Ireland.”My Department has not provided any other funding to Cricket Ireland, the Northern Cricket Union or the North West Cricket Union in the last twelve months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding that has been provided to Cricket Ireland, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 29881/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with the Irish Football Association in relation to the potential expansion of Windsor Park in advance of 2035 Women’s World Cup.
answer_text: As the bidding process for the 2035 FIFA Women’s World Cup remains in its early stages, I have not had any formal discussions with the Irish Football Association regarding the potential expansion of Windsor Park in advance of the tournament.	The Football Associations of Northern Ireland, England, Scotland and Wales have submitted a joint Expression of Interest to FIFA to host the tournament in 2035.  It has since been confirmed that this is the only valid Expression of Interest received by FIFA for the tournament.  A formal bid is expected to be submitted by the end of 2025, and it is anticipated that FIFA will announce the successful host nation in mid-2026.	My officials will continue to monitor developments and will engage with the Irish Football Association and other stakeholders as planning progresses and further information becomes available, including any clarification of FIFA’s technical requirements for host venues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to provide an update on capital funding opportunities to upgrade facilities at Northern Ireland’s Premier League cricket grounds.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:	Sport NI do not currently have any capital funding programme opportunities open that could be used to upgrade facilities at Northern Ireland’s Premier League cricket grounds.		Sport NI is currently developing a new capital investment fund focusing on improving the quality of facilities and equipment at club and community sports facilities. While the final criterion is still being confirmed, we expect it to be grants up to £50,000 with a partnership funding requirement. Support will be available to community or voluntary groups who can demonstrate sport and physical activity as a primary purpose, alongside evidence of property ownership or a long-term lease. A level of partnership funding will also be required. We hope to launch this fund later in the summer, and details will be published on our website and social media channels once available.	Any further capital or infrastructure programmes that Sport NI open will be shared on our website and social media channels and Sport NI would recommend interested parties monitor Sport NI’s website and follow Sport NI’s social media channels for future funding programmes that they may be able to avail themselves of.My Department does not have any other capital funding opportunities for Premier League cricket grounds.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when guidance will be available within her Department to process individual requests to install a domestic EV charging cable across a public footpath for those without off-street parking.
answer_text: My Department has considered solutions that may be possible. This work has focused on two solutions; an engineered solution which requires the opening of the footway, and cable protectors which are already widely used to prevent tripping hazards and are designed not to obstruct the footway for users including those with disabilities. The provision of these solutions is complex from a legislative and public safety perspective.This work is nearing completion, and I hope to be in a position to provide more information in the near future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide an estimated timescale for the publication of a final strategy for the remediation of the Mobuoy site.
answer_text: Any timetable for publication of a final remediation is subject to the outcome of the public consultation. A 16-week consultation on the draft Remediation Strategy for the Mobuoy site launched on the 13 June 2025 and will close on 2 October 2025. Following the consultation, responses will be considered, and my officials will determine any necessary amendments to the draft strategy and the time required to complete any changes.A subsequent decision will be required for the adoption of the strategy, and I will need to carefully consider DAERA’s statutory duties in protecting water quality, and the wider role of the Executive and Assembly in the scrutiny and affordability, in the context of the constraints of public sector finances.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the current estimated cost for the remediation of the Mobuoy site; and (ii) when the business case will be completed.
answer_text: There is no agreed preferred remediation option for the Mobuoy site or current estimated cost for remediation and there will not be until the public consultation has been completed. A business case will be developed following the completion of the consultation process.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on (i) the Proposed Ammonia Strategy and Revised Operational Protocol (ii) the anticipated timeframes in relation to when any new policy may be implemented.
answer_text: An eight-week consultation on the Environmental Report for the Proposed Ammonia Strategy and the Revised Operational Protocol to assess the impacts of air pollution on the natural environment, as part of the process of Strategic Environmental Assessment (SEA), concluded on 23 May 2025. Responses were also invited on the associated documents including the Update on the Proposed Ammonia Strategy and the Revised Operational Protocol.The current public consultation on the proposed Nutrients Action Programme (NAP) for 2026 – 2029 is seeking views on two elements of the proposed Ammonia Strategy. These are the proposed step by step move for all farms in Northern Ireland to use Low Emission Slurry Spreading Equipment by 2030, and on a prohibition on the use of granular urea fertilisers unless they contain urease inhibitors. I have stated that my intention is to convene a stakeholder task and finish group to finalise the draft NAP proposed measures, following which I will launch a second consultation. Following this NAP consultation, all responses will be considered and will inform production of the final SEA Statement, Operational Protocol and Ammonia Strategy. The anticipated timeframe for implementation of the new Ammonia policies will be determined by the finalisation of the NAP for 2026 – 2029.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of applications for a rent freeze that have been received by the Housing Executive in the last ten years; and (ii) what financial impact this has had on the income of the organisation.
answer_text: NIHE’s rent increase requests are considered by my Department on an annual basis. A rent freeze has been applied to Housing Executive rents in 5 out of the last 10 financial years spanning the period 2016/17 to 2025/26, namely 2016/17, 2017/18, 2018/19, 2019/20 and 2022/23.  If NIHE rents had increased by CPI (inflation) in the years in which the rent was frozen, then additional income of circa £199.6m would have been generated over the 10-year period.  It is important to note that the impact of a rent freeze is not limited to the year in which it is applied: it also serves to reduce rental income by an equivalent amount in subsequent years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail a breakdown of the percentage of people in receipt of Housing Benefit who have been placed in temporary accommodation, in the past year.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows: ‘‘Due to the transient nature of homelessness, the Housing Executive is unable to accurately detail a breakdown of the percentage of people in receipt of Housing Benefit who have been placed in temporary accommodation in the past year; however, on 1 June 2025 approximately 90% of people placed in temporary accommodation received support with their housing costs through Housing Benefit.’’

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail whether there are any current plans to source funding to restore the Martin Memorial Clock in Shrigley.
answer_text: The Department and NIHE will continue to explore opportunities to source funding for the Martin Memorial Clock Tower in Shrigley, however at present no funding has yet been secured for its restoration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the methodology and the data sets used to determine which farms were over the 150kgN/ha limit and classified as more intensive farms under the Consultation on the Nutrients Actions Programme 2026-2029.
answer_text: Total nitrogen (N) production can be calculated for a farm by multiplying livestock numbers by the relevant N excretion rate for the specific type and age of livestock. The N per hectare value is determined by dividing the total N production value by the total area farmed. For farms with cattle, estimated total N production from cattle per hectare per year was calculated based on cattle numbers recorded on the Animal and Public Health Information System (APHIS) on 1st June 2023 and total farmed area recorded on the 2023 Agricultural Census.  This enabled farms with estimated annual cattle manure N production at or above 150kg N/ha per year to be identified.For poultry, a working assumption was made that farms with at least 20,000 birds have an estimated annual livestock manure nitrogen production at or above 150kg N/ha per year. For pigs, a working assumption was made that farms with at least 400 pigs have an estimated annual livestock manure nitrogen production at or above 150 kg N/ha per year. The number of these poultry and pig farms was derived from the 2023 Agricultural Census. The assumptions for the size of poultry and pig farms have been set on the basis that enterprises at and above these levels are considered more likely to exceed the 150 kg N/ha per year threshold.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of (i) the number of 5G telecom masts which have been destroyed in the last four years; and (ii) the impact this has on consumers.
answer_text: I condemn the wanton destruction of this essential communications infrastructure. It is despicable and has serious consequences for local communities, businesses and public services. These attacks leave citizens and businesses without connection or with limited connection until the damage has been repaired and have potential to increase costs to services and limit the ability of operators to invest in improvements to their networks.  They have also required our emergency services to divert resources to respond to these attacks. I am aware that the mast attacks are under investigation by the PSNI, and I would urge anyone with any information to pass it on to the police. I understand mobile industry representatives are liaising with the PSNI to help improve security around this essential infrastructure. Mobile infrastructure is privately owned and therefore my Department does not routinely gather this data however Mobile UK, the trade association for the Mobile Network Operators has advised that the following arson attacks on mobile infrastructure have occurred in the past four years – 2022, 3 attacks; 2023, 6 attacks; 2024, 11 attacks; and 2025, 5 attacks. I am scheduled to meet with mobile industry representatives later this month, the mast attacks will be discussed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the amount his Department contributed to the budget for Northern Ireland Housing Executive housing adaptations, in each of last 10 years.
answer_text: My Department has not contributed to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive’s budget for housing adaptations in any of the last 10 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any plans he has to increase the powers of local councils in relation to housing safety.
answer_text: The New Residential Building Safety Policy is still under development, and it would not be appropriate to share full details at this stage.  However, my officials are actively reviewing the regulatory framework and exploring options to enhance safety outcomes across local and central government.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether any consideration has been given to granting powers to local councils to vary the level of non-domestic rate, similar to the standard non-domestic rate and small business domestic rate in operation in other parts of the UK.
answer_text: ANSWERLocal government finance, a major part of which is the setting of district rates by District Councils, is a matter that falls within the policy competence of the Minister for Communities. I can, however, consider any proposals that the Minister may bring to the wider Executive in relation to this area.The Transforming Business Rates proposals, being worked on in England and Wales to apply a reduced level poundage for small businesses are predicated on their distinct model of a uniform business rate poundage. Here, we have two elements to our rate poundage, the regional rate set by the Executive and the district rate set by the local council. Due to the differences in the two systems, the proposals for Transforming Business Rates are being looked at through a local lens in the context of the Strategic Review of Rating on Small Business Rate Relief. This is currently being undertaken by my Department. A link to the Terms of Reference is provided below:-https://www.finance-ni.gov.uk/publications/strategic-review-rating-terms-reference

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 10525/22-27 and AQW 12010/22-27, to detail the average time of responses to pre-application discussions applications by DfI Southern Division in Ards and North Down Borough Council area, in each of the last 12 months.
answer_text: At present, and due to other critical priorities within my Department, my officials are only able to respond to regionally significant Pre Application Discussions (PADs) applications in this area.The Department does not measure average response time to pre-application discussion (PAD) applications but instead measures the number of responses issued within a specific time frame.

answer_text: Ambulance call categorisation for emergency calls is standardised across the UK.  For your information, ambulance call category definitions and response time standards are set out in Table 1 below. question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 13600/22-27, for an update on the transition of the 1,065 citizens who were receiving telecare services through Health and Social Care contracts to find alternative providers.
answer_text: Telecoms is a reserved matter and guidance on the transition has been published by UK Government at https://www.gov.uk/guidance/uk-transition-from-analogue-to-digital-landlines. OFCOM, the regulator for this area has also published information on the changeover at www.ofcom.org.uk/phones-and-broadbands/landline-phones/upgrading-landlines-to-digial-technology . Telecommunications companies are replacing the technology they use to provide fixed telephone networks (landlines) with this work currently scheduled to complete by January 2027. It is their responsibility to ensure that those who depend on a landline are provided with a resilient solution. Currently 2,340 citizens receive telecare services through HSC contracts.  To date 1,704 (72%) have transitioned to a digital device and my officials have been working with the telecare service provider to complete this work by January 2027.  There are plans in place with telecommunications providers to transition the remaining 636 telecare users to digital devices. There is no requirement to find alternative providers, unless citizens are choosing to change to an alternative service provider of their own volition.My Chief Digital Information Officer is aware of the issues in this area and regular contact is maintained with colleagues in the other jurisdictions and with the Technology Enabled Care Services Association, to remain abreast of developments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what plans she has to ensure that victims and persons accused of a crime are kept separate and not able to access or occupy the same spaces in Newtownards Court.
answer_text: There are separate waiting areas set aside for victims and witnesses at Newtownards Courthouse. It is also possible for a vulnerable and/or intimidated witness, who are eligible for special measures, to give evidence via live link from a room within the courthouse or from a Remote Evidence Centre.  The Victim & Witness Waiting Rooms, Video Link Rooms and the Remote Evidence Centres are staffed by the Victim Support NI Witness Service and the NSPCC Young Witness Service.  Court Security Officers are also available should they be needed to assist victims and witnesses while attending court.The Victim Support NI Witness Service and the NSPCC Young Witness Service ensure that all prosecution witnesses and victims are offered emotional support and practical information. This includes a chance to visit the court or Remote Evidence Centre before giving evidence, explaining the court process, accompanying the victim or witness into the courtroom or live link room, and offering the opportunity to talk when the case has ended.It is recognised that service users encounter functional deficiencies and barriers within many court buildings. The Estate Strategy, alongside supporting data analysis, will be used to establish evidence-based strategic asset management plans and to inform future investment requirements. Through incremental delivery of the Estate Strategy, Vision 2030 will ensure that court and tribunal buildings are fit for purpose and can support physical, virtual and hybrid services and provide a consistent standard of accommodation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for an update on the work of the UK Deposit Management Organisation; and (ii) to detail the projected cost for the implementation of the deposit return scheme.
answer_text: UK Deposit Management Organisation (DMO) were appointed on 06 May 2025 as the operator of the Deposit Return Scheme (DRS) in England and Northern Ireland. The Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland (Designation of Scheme Administrator) Order 2025 became law on 20 June 2025, designating UK DMO as the scheme administrator for Scotland’s DRS. UK DMO is an independent, business-led organisation appointed to develop, and ultimately deliver the scheme day-to-day. Importantly the DMO role is to create the necessary infrastructure and to run the Deposit Return Scheme (DRS). This means managing producer registrations, flow of deposits, financial obligations, effective collection of drinks containers and ensuring that recycling rates improve over time. Following appointment, UK DMO is now focused on establishing itself as an organisation to implement and run DRS on behalf of industry in England, Northern Ireland and Scotland. Work areas currently being progressed include DMO recruitment, communications, organisation set up and stakeholder engagement. UK DMO is a not-for-profit organisation whose operations form part of the overall costs of running DRS, which are funded by industry.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail to what extent her Department will be liable for in relation to unfulfilled contractual obligations, following the High Court judgement in relation to the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.    to detail to what extent her Department will be liable for unfulfilled contractual obligations, following the High Court judgement in relation to the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.
answer_text: The main construction contracts for the A5 Western Transport Corridor were set up under a 2-phase mechanism, whereby Phase 2, that would enable full construction works to commence, would only be entered into on successful completion of all statutory processes including legal challenges.  Phase 2 was not entered into. Therefore, there are no unfulfilled contractual obligations in relation to the construction contracts for the A5WTC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has undertaken ,or commissioned, any research into the potential impact of wildfire or gorse fire related badger displacement on the incidence of bovine TB.
answer_text: My Department has neither undertaken nor commissioned research into the potential impact of wildfire or gorsefire related badger displacement on the incidence of bovine tuberculosis (bTB).Scientific research plays an important role to further understand and effectively address bTB maintenance and spread.  DAERA has commissioned a range of bTB related science research projects over the past number of years, through the Agrifood and Biosciences Institute. Current research projects include investigating bTB transmission pathways and the analysis of Mycobacterium bovis strain types in NI.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 26213/22-27, to clarify where farmers now stand given that the land vested by the Department for Infrastructure is to be returned to them following the quashing of the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.
answer_text: My officials continue to liaise closely with their counterparts in the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) on the implications for DAERA of the recent court ruling, including in relation to land previously used for agricultural activity.  I am aware that DfI colleagues will be writing to the affected landowners shortly to agree a way forward.  Once my Department has more information on that way forward, it will be better placed to advise on this matter.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there will be a compensation scheme for farmers who had land vested, pursuant to the quashed decision to commence the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme, in relation to lost grants, earnings and other loss.
answer_text: My Officials and LPS are currently considering options.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent by her Department in defending the most recent judicial review proceedings, in relation to the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.
answer_text: It is too early to establish the amount spent in defending the most recent judicial review proceedings, in relation to the A5 Western Transport Corridor.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 13922/22-27, for an update on  any telephone calls, meetings or other communications he has had with (i) the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs; (ii) the Minister of State for Food, Farming and Fisheries; and (iii) the Minister for Environment, Food and Agriculture in the Isle of Man, in relation to the closure of fishing grounds to Northern Ireland fishermen by the Isle of Man government, in the last twelve months.
answer_text: Following Isle of Man Government’s announcement for plans to introduce new fisheries spatial management measures in the Western Irish Sea Mud-belt, I met with Clare Barber, Minister for the Department of Environment, Food and Agriculture, Isle of Man on 17th June 2024 to discuss these measures and the impact that they were having on the Northern Ireland Fishing sector.  I have not had any direct meetings with the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs; the Minister of State for Food, Farming and Fisheries; or the Minister for Environment, Food and Agriculture in the Isle of Man, in relation to the closure of fishing grounds to Northern Ireland fishermen by the Isle of Man government, in the last twelve months. However, at a meeting with yourself and representatives from the Northern Ireland fishing industry on 13th February 2025, it was agreed that DAERA officials would visit the Isle of Man to discuss closures, Irish Sea science and offshore wind. This visit took place on 25th June 2025. Officials discussed a range of issues including the new spatial management measures in Isle of Man territorial waters. The discussions were highly constructive, with both sides recognising significant common ground on shared challenges and opportunities.This meeting marks a positive step forward in strengthening cross-jurisdictional cooperation in the Irish Sea, with a shared focus on sustainability, equity, and evidence-based policy. My officials will be working closely with Isle of Man officials going forward and will be meeting regularly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in light of the High Court ruling of 23 June 2025 on the A5 Western Transport Corridor Scheme, whether he intends to review climate change legislation, including targets, in order to enable the delivery of major infrastructure projects.
answer_text: I and my officials are considering the detailed High Court judgement and will be engaging with the Department for Infrastructure, to assist them, as they work through the outworkings of the ruling. I recognise the importance of the A5 Western Transport Scheme, and I remain supportive of the previous decision to progress the scheme in the interests of road safety and the higher-level objective of balancing regional infrastructure.  The challenge now is for the Executive to work together to deliver our climate responsibilities in a manner that will help households, communities, and businesses to reduce emissions, improve health and well-being, grasp green economic opportunities and build long term resilience.As Minister I am focused on delivering our legislative obligations to tackle Climate Change and grasping the benefits of decarbonisation rather than reneging on our commitments to tackle Climate Change as set out in legislation passed unanimously by the Assembly at Final Stage only a few years ago.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will be investigating how a decision to proceed with the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme was allowed to be made without satisfactory evidence to show that it would not prevent the mandatory greenhouse gas emissions targets being met, as advised by the Planning Appeals Commission, following the Public Local Inquiries into the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.
answer_text: The Departments responses to PAC’s Recommendations on mandatory greenhouse gas emissions targets is dealt with in the Departmental Statement 2024 Recommendations 14 – 17. starting at paragraph 5.3.16.  This is available on https://www.a5wtc.com/pages/departmental-statements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the net zero targets, as set out in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, will prevent any further progress on the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme and other major infrastructure projects, including the Ballynahinch bypass.
answer_text: The judgement was extremely detailed and it was the first legal test for the Climate Change Act and for Climate Action Plan – something every department will now have to deal with the outworkings of.  We are working through the judgement and it is too soon to conclude on the impact to the A5 Western Transport Corridor and wider infrastructure projects.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given the recent High Court judgement on the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme, whether the vesting process will now cease in respect of the lands affected by this scheme.
answer_text: The judge quashed the decision and the orders on the 27 June 2025. The Department will work with all landowners.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps her Department took to show that the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme, if proceeded with, would not prevent the mandatory greenhouse gas emissions targets being met, as per the Planning Appeals Commission report, following the Public Local Inquiries into the A5 Western Transport Corridor scheme.
answer_text: The steps taken by my Department to address the mandatory greenhouse gas emissions targets are detailed in the Departmental Statement 2024 Recommendations 14 – 17 starting at paragraph 5.3.16.  This is available on https://www.a5wtc.com/pages/departmental-statements.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he anticipates the £70 million capital investment required for the agriculture sector, as outlined within the draft Climate Action Plan 2023-2027, will be made by his Department or industry.
answer_text: The £70 million capital investment required for the agriculture sector for the 2023-2027 draft Climate Action Plan (CAP) focuses on the projected level of investment required from the Northern Ireland Executive budget to support the achievement of the quantified emission reductions. It relates to capital spend on a number of the Sustainable Agriculture Programme Schemes over the CAP period including the Bovine Genetics Project, Soil Nutrient Health Scheme, Farming with Nature, Beef Sustainability Package, Carbon Footprinting Project and Livestock Dietary Emissions Challenge Fund. All values provided are in line with the Central Scenario criteria, which assumes that credible funding arrangements are in place or there is high confidence of funding availability.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail what barriers there are to progressing renewable energy development on Forest Service land.
answer_text: The Forestry Act (Northern Ireland) 2010 (The Act) provides the powers under which Forest Service land may be developed for a non forestry land use, which can include renewable energy projects. The Act however, requires that the Department must give due regard to its primary duty to manage its forests sustainably in considering such developments.   Responses to the Wind Energy Market Engagement exercise launched 6 March 2025 identified a number of potential barriers that may arise in some circumstances. Amongst these are: proximity of grid connection and timing constraints; complexities around planning and ecological considerations including designations and ornithological assessments; and protected landscapes and heritage constraints.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail how he plans to fund the proposals outlined within the draft Climate Change Action Plan 2023-2027 Consultation.
answer_text: Funding proposals contained in the draft Climate Action Plan is the responsibility of the entire Northern Ireland Executive. 		Approximately £226m capital has already been invested.  Some costs will fall to DAERA, whilst other costs will fall to other departments. Normal budgeting processes apply to ensure that the costs as well as the opportunities of addressing climate change are considered as part of economic appraisals.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline (i) why the Capital Investment Scheme under the agriculture pillar of the draft Climate Change Action Plan 2023-2027 Consultation has been classed as unquantified; and (ii) how this will be delivered.
answer_text: The proposed Capital Investment Scheme within the Sustainable Agriculture Programme will help farm businesses improve both their environmental performance and business efficiency. It is proposed that support will focus on assisting the industry to meet Climate Change targets and to help improve air and water quality. This will be achieved by the adoption of precision technology and equipment to reduce ammonia emissions, carbon emissions, and nutrient loss. It will build on information provided to farm businesses through the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme and the Carbon Footprinting Project.  It is proposed that the Capital Investment Scheme will offer support for a range of technology and equipment.  It will not be possible to quantify the scheme’s contribution to carbon reduction until analysis is undertaken of the specific items selected by applicants for funding. Development of the scheme is ongoing, with co-design through stakeholder organisations, and it is not possible to set out precisely at this stage how the proposed scheme will be delivered.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the engagement he had with the agricultural sector on the draft Climate Change Action Plan 2023-2027 Consultation (i) during the development of the Plan; and (ii) prior to its publication.
answer_text: My officials have formally engaged with the sector during the development of the agriculture sector input to the draft Climate Action Plan on 15 occasions since 2021.  This has comprised presentations and seeking views at five separate meetings of the Agricultural Policy Stakeholders Group.  A further ten separate engagement events were held with a range of sector groups either at arranged meetings or at sectoral conferences.  At its  meeting on 11 June 2025 the Agricultural Policy Stakeholder Group was advised that the consultation on the draft Climate Action Plan 2023-2027 would be launched during w/c 16 June 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of herds, that were restricted from moving animals as a result of a TB outbreak, that requested to move animals to another holding under licence in the last twelve months.
answer_text: DAERA does not hold collated information on the total number of herds, that were restricted from moving animals as a result of a TB outbreak, which requested to move animals to another holding under licence in the last 12 months.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the percentage of animals that had TB detected during a TB test, that were animals of less than 42 days old, in the last twelve months.
answer_text: Data for the 12 months up to 30 April 2025 indicates that there were five calves, under the age of 42 days detected as skin test positive during a TB test. These calves were all in the same herd.  This represents 0.024% of skin test reactors. 	Animals less than 42 days old are not routinely tested at TB tests. However, they may be tested in herds when the disease situation in a herd indicates an increased risk to young calves.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of herds, that were restricted from moving as a result of a TB outbreak, were approved to move animals under licence to another herd, in the last twelve months.
answer_text: From the 12 months from June 2024 to May 2025, there were 27 distinct herds that, while being restricted from moving animals as a result of a TB outbreak, were approved to move animals under licence to another non-associated herd.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the average processing time in the last twelve months for dealing with requests to move animals under licence for herds that are restricted from moving animals following a TB outbreak.
answer_text: In the 12 months from June 2024 to May 2025, the average processing time for moving animals under licence, for herds that are restricted from moving animals as a result of a TB outbreak:for moves to meat plants was within the same working day.  (ii) for moves to other non-associated herds was an average of 21 calendar days, from the date the exceptional move was requested until an approval/rejection decision was given.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any representations he has made to the UK Government on behalf of the Northern Ireland fishing fleet in relation to Seasonal Worker visas, in the last twelve months.
answer_text: UK Immigration Policy is an excepted matter and therefore I do not have the remit to deliver changes to the Seasonal Agricultural Workers Scheme. In response to Parliamentary Questions and representations by the Fishermen’s Welfare Alliance in early 2024, the previous UK Government made it clear that fishing and fish processing occupations were classed as non-seasonal and therefore ineligible for the Seasonal Agricultural Workers Visa route. The Migration Advisory Committee (MAC) also did not recommend that the scope of the Seasonal Workers Visa should be extended, according to their Inquiry into the visa route, published in July 2024.  Following the publication of the MAC’s review, I wrote to Professor Brian Bell, the Chair of the MAC, noting that the conclusions would continue to restrict the ability of the fishing industry to use the route and emphasised that a labour solution needs be found for our fishing sector, until such time that automation is more advanced and accessible.I have recently communicated in detail the issues faced by NI agri-food and fisheries businesses with regards to inadequate labour availability with the Defra Secretary of State, the Home Secretary and the Minister for Migration and Citizenship. In my letter, I provided feedback on the UK Government’s Immigration White Paper following its publication on 12th May 2025 and reiterated immigration policy proposals which would provide solutions to these challenges, including those put forward by the NI Fishermen’s Federation (NIFF). I await a response from Defra and the Home Office. My Department is working with the Department of the Economy in relation to making further representations to the Home Secretary on issues arising from the White Paper.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to provide a timescale for the establishment of a dedicated co-design group with a remit to develop a suite of support measures for the sheep sector.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within her Department.
answer_text: The Department engaged Innovation & Consultancy Services (ICS) to undertake a programme of independent reviews of six Departmental Arm’s Length Bodies (ALBs). Four of these reviews have been fully completed, with the remaining two expected to be completed in July 2025.  The Department has also commenced a programme of work which will be taken forward in conjunction with Further Education Colleges over the next 24 months, to review the Further Education delivery model.  The programme will promote greater consistency in the provision of technical and vocational educational pathways, as well as innovation support for employers in pursuit of my Economic Vision.The review of Tourism NI will be undertaken, once its new CEO is in post.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within her Department.
answer_text: The New Decade New Approach agreement included a commitment which focused on an efficiency and effectiveness review of Arm’s Length Bodies (ALBs). Across my Department, there is a continual emphasis on driving this throughout the ALBs. This has been particularly important in the context of constrained budget outcomes, whereby there has been insufficient funding to deliver everything we would like. This is ongoing and delivered through robust challenge and engagement. At this stage, through this drive for efficiency and effectiveness, no areas for reform have been identified.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the review of arm's-length bodies being undertaken within their Department.
To ask the Minister for Communities how his Department is ensuring that Small Pockets of Deprivation funding is allocated equitably.
answer_text: Firstly, I note the Members’ interest in the Small Pockets of Deprivation funding which is managed by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive on behalf of my Department.You will be aware from my recent budget announcement; I announced that I have provided an additional £2.8 million for the Voluntary and Community Sector. My Department is committed to ensuring that the sector availing of the Small Pockets of Deprivation funding, has been allocated fairly and proportionately.To this end, those organisations currently in receipt of the Small Pockets of Deprivation funding will not only continue to receive their equitable share but will benefit from a 9% increase for the 2025/2026 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current waiting time for a car driving test.
answer_text: The average waiting time for a Category B (private car) driving test across the Driver and Vehicle Agency’s (DVA) network of test centres, from 1 April 2025 to 19 June 2025, is 29 days. This represents unvalidated management information taken from DVA’s booking system on 20 June 2025.  Waiting times regularly fluctuate based on customer demand and the availability of staff resources to conduct driving tests.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within her Department.
answer_text: To date, only a review of the Probation Board for Northern Ireland and the Criminal Justice Inspection NI have been completed.  Although action plans had been developed by my Department in respect of its other ALBs resourcing pressures and other emerging priorities have held up further progress being made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within his Department.
answer_text: Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) - A review of AFBI has recently been carried out. A formal response to the report and its recommendations are currently being considered.NI Fishery Harbour Authority (NIFHA) - A review has recently been undertaken. The findings and recommendations will be considered in conjunction with NIFHA.    Livestock and Meat Commission for Northern Ireland (LMC) - A review is planned to be undertaken in 2025/26.Loughs Agency – As a North South Body Loughs Agency is not in scope for standard reviews. Loughs Agency was last reviewed in 2008, following a commitment made in The St Andrews Agreement, October 2006 to review all North South Implementation Bodies.Agricultural Wages Board (AWB) - As AWB has no staff or budget, a light-touch review is planned in 2026/27.Council for Nature Conservation and the Countryside (CNCC) - Consideration is currently being given to carrying out a review of CNCC.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within his Department.
answer_text: In line with the New Decade, New Approach commitment, my Department has commissioned, and published, reviews for three of its Arm’s Length Bodies to date – the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Children and Young People (NICCY), the Commissioner for Older People for Northern Ireland (COPNI) and the Northern Ireland Local Government Officers Superannuation Committee (NILGOSC).  Consideration is currently being given to further reviews in line with the New Decade, New Approach commitment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within his Department.
answer_text: I have already determined that the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland (GTCNI) should be retained and reformed and I intend to legislate on this basis. In addition, a “Landscape Review” of the Education Authority (EA) was completed in June 2022. I have no plans for further reviews of either organisation during the current mandate.My department is reviewing the need for other Landscape Reviews on a case-by-case basis. The commissioning of other Landscape Reviews will be subject to staff and budgetary constraints.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within his Department.
answer_text: ANSWERMy Department has two sponsored bodies that would fall within scope of the review of Arm’s Length Bodies following New Decade, New Approach. These are the Fiscal Council and the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner, both of which are new creations.The Fiscal Council was established on a non-statutory basis in 2021.  The Council’s role and duties have been extensively reviewed in the development of the Fiscal Council Bill.The office of the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner will be formally established in September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the review of arm’s-length bodies being undertaken within his Department.
answer_text: I can advise that the work on the review of ALBs is to be taken forward as part of the Department's reset plans.  The rate of progress of any reviews will be subject to staff and budgetary constraints.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of memorials to terrorists on property owned by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies, which have been removed in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Roadside memorials comprise many different formats from permanent structures to temporary floral tributes and given the transient nature of many of these memorials, my Department does not maintain records of their location. As such we are unable at any given time to quantify how many memorials have been removed in each of the last five years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the amount of refuse-derived fuel exported from Northern Ireland in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The figures for Refuse Derived Fuel (RDF) exported from Northern Ireland are published on the DAERA website below.	https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/export-records-rdf-shipped-northern-ireland
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of meetings of the Coastal Forum, of which she is Co-Chair, have taken place since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: There have been no meetings of the Coastal Forum since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.I recently discussed this matter with the Minister for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs and we have agreed that a Coastal Forum meeting should be arranged for autumn this year.  This will be preceded by a meeting of the Coastal Forum working group over the summer period to review progress that has been made on the Coastal Forum work programme and to prepare agenda items for the Coastal Forum meeting.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of meetings of the Coastal Forum that have taken place since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: Whilst there have been no meetings of the Coastal Forum since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024 I recently discussed this matter with the Minister for Infrastructure and we have agreed that a Coastal Forum meeting should be arranged for autumn this year.  This will be preceded by a meeting of the Coastal Forum working group over the summer period to review progress that has been made on the Coastal Forum work programme and to prepare agenda items for the Coastal Forum meeting.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what consideration has his Department given to the wider sustainability and reputational implications for Northern Ireland of its increasing reliance on waste exports.
answer_text: Overall, the figures for total waste exports do not show an upward trend. Waste exports include Refuse Derived Fuel (RDF) and exports of RDF are increasing.RDF is produced from residual waste after many recyclable materials have been removed. It contains calorific value and can be used as fuel for incineration to produce energy from waste. Energy recovery is the next best option for waste that cannot be further recycled and in most case is preferable to landfill.Northern Ireland does not currently have sufficient infrastructure to use the volumes of RDF generated. It is therefore exported under EU regulations on shipments of waste to countries which have the facilities and capacity to use it to produce energy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

answer_text: Roadside memorials comprise many different formats from permanent structures to temporary floral tributes and given the transient nature of many of these memorials, my Department does not maintain records of their location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline (i) how many requests were received for a mandatory reconsideration of a Personal Independence Payment decision in the last five years; and (ii) how many decisions (a) remained the same; and (b) were changed, in that period.
answer_text: The most recent Personal Independence Payment (PIP) statistics were published in May 2025 and covered the period up to 28 February 2025. In the five years up to 28th February 2025, a total of 44,990 mandatory reconsiderations were requested.  (a)  29,980 (67%) of these resulted in the decision and award     remaining unchanged; and(b) 12,790 (28%) resulted in a different decision and a revised award.  The remaining 2,360 (5%) mandatory reconsiderations were withdrawn or cancelled.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) how many Universal Credit claimants have been overpaid cost of living payments; and (ii) the total amount of the overpayments identified.
answer_text: The table below provides the information requested: 	No. of Universal Credit claimants with a cost of living overpayment @18.6.25	Total value of cost of living overpayments for these claimants  1,801£744k		The information contained in the above table is taken from operational data systems and is not intended for publication. Therefore, the data is not quality assured to the standard of published Official Statistics.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of complaints received regarding the quality of telephone assessments carried out by Capita, for all applicable benefit types in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Capita have been responsible for conducting PIP assessments in NI from 2016. Since September 2024, Capita also conduct all other health assessments. Capita have advised that, in line with its data retention policy, complaint records are only held for two years. Since June 2023, there have been 519 Personal Independence Payment complaints where a telephone assessment has taken place. Since September 2024 there have been 12 Universal Credit and 7 Employment and Support Allowance complaints, where a telephone assessment has taken place. However, Capita have confirmed that none of the above complaints specifically relate to the quality of the telephone assessment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQO 2062/22-27, (i) how many of the 11 local planning authorities have completed the Local Development Plan process within the 40 months, identified in the Strategic Planning Policy Statement, published by her Department in 2015; and (ii) whether she will reconsider her assessment of there having been good progress.
answer_text: There is no legislative timeframe for Councils to bring forward their Local Development Plans (LDPs). While a diagram within the Strategic Planning Policy Statement (SPPS), suggested 40 months to bring forward both a Plan Strategy and a Local Policies Plan, this was produced for indicative purposes only and not as a target. No council has completed the LDP process within this time frame.Whilst good progress has been made, multiple factors have contributed to the time taken in bringing forward LDPs as we move towards bringing a full cycle of Plans through the system. Prior to publishing their LDP, each council must take the time to undertake rigorous surveying of their area to develop a robust evidence base upon which to build their emerging planning policies. This will vary from council to council.I am determined that we need to build further upon the progress made to date in relation to progressing council LDPs. As we enter phase 2 of the Planning Improvement Programme stakeholders including my Department, councils and statutory consultees are committed to learning from the collective experience of the LDP process to date and using that knowledge to foster continuous improvement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQO 2062/22-27, for her assessment of whether the timescale of 40 months to complete a Local Development Plan, as identified in the Strategic Planning Policy Statement, was an achievable target.
answer_text: There is no legislative timeframe for Councils to bring forward their Local Development Plans. While a diagram within the Strategic Planning Policy Statement (SPPS), suggested 40 months to bring forward both a Plan Strategy and a Local Policies Plan, this was produced for indicative purposes only and not as a target.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of parking tickets issued; (ii) the number of parking tickets challenged; and (iii) the number of successful challenges of parking tickets issued, at the Ulster Hospital, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Parking enforcement, including the issue of parking tickets, is provided by a 3rd party licenced contractor at the Ulster Hospital Site. The South Eastern HSC Trust do not own, control or process enforcement data captured by 3rd party licenced contractors.  The current parking enforcement contractor has advised that they are unable to release the enforcement information requested due to it being assessed as commercially sensitive.The previous parking enforcement contractor has permitted the Trust to release the total number of parking tickets issued, at the Ulster Hospital, in each of the last 5 calendar years as follows:  2020: 933 Parking Tickets issued; 2021: 1,488 Parking Tickets issued; 2022: 3,878 Parking Tickets issued; 2023: 3,129 Parking Tickets issued and 2024: 2,435 Parking Tickets issued.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to transferring additional powers and responsibilities to local councils.
answer_text: I would refer the member to the answer to AQW 9124/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 6905/22-27, for an update on plans to increase the provision of NHS dentists in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: Access to General Dental Services remains challenging across Northern Ireland, and indeed the UK. Part of the reason for this is the remuneration paid to dentists for their work. For many months now, the Department has been attempting to address this issue with targeted investments.For 2024/25 a £9.2m investment package supported access to General Dental Services, including:£1m for newly registered child patients through the reestablishment of the Enhanced Child Examination Scheme;£4.3m to fund a 30% enhancement to fees paid to dentists for priority treatments; and£3.9m for the treatment of high priority unregistered patients through a Dental Access Scheme.To assist with the increase to National Insurance contributions, £2.5million has been made available for General Dental Services.Last month I announced a further £7m investment for 2025/26 to continue supporting the industry until reform can be progressed. The investment will fund continuation of the Enhanced Child Examination Scheme and 30% enhancement on fees paid to dentists for Health Service fillings, extractions, and root canals, alongside a new £1.6m allocation to provide additional support to dental practitioners who continue to provide Health Service dental care for their patients.Further, funding is being provided to expand the Happy Smiles Programme to include Primary 1, 2, and 3 children. Happy Smiles was originally launched in 2016 with the aim of improving the oral health of nursery school children in the 20% most deprived areas in Northern Ireland.While I am pleased to have been able to announce this investment, it has been made in the face of a £600m deficit for my Department, which has severely limited my ability to implement the kind of transformative change we would all like to see.Longer term, my Department’s Health and Social Care NI three-year plan includes a commitment to develop an action plan and programme of work for the reform of General Dental Services by April 2027.The first step on this path is to determine the real cost of providing dental services so that realistic options for reform of the service can be developed.To achieve that I have approved the commissioning of a cost-of-service review to be completed in 2025/26.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current priority list for traffic calming measures in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: Traffic calming schemes are funded through my Department’s Local Transport Safety Measures (LTSM) budget which covers a range of measures aimed at providing a safer road transport system. Requests are considered in line with current policies and guidance and any works are subject to prioritisation with all viable proposals competing for limited resource.  Regrettably, the available resources, both funding and staff is insufficient to deliver all schemes that have been assessed as worthwhile.My Department prepares priority lists of traffic calming measures and annual planned programmes of work are presented to respective Councils when budgets have been confirmed.The priority list of traffic calming measures is not aligned to Constituency area and as traffic calming measures covers a wide range of requests, any attempt to provide you with the information requested would require an extensive exercise to determine the number related solely to the Strangford constituency and would very likely involve disproportionate costs.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding should be directed to the PSNI using their non-emergency 101 number.One of the best ways to ensure the safety of our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury on roads by 2050 is by changing driver behaviour.  As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.  The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a breakdown of the amount spent by DfI Roads on (i) resurfacing; and (ii) maintenance in the towns of (a) Newtownards; (b) Comber; (c) Portaferry; (d) Saintfield; and (e) Ballynahinch, in the last five years.
answer_text: My Department does not hold information in the format requested, however details of Capital and Resource Structural Maintenance expenditure on the roads infrastructure by District Council for each of the last five financial years available is set out in the Table below. Resource Structural Maintenance includes patching of carriageway and footways.Expenditure will have been determined by budget availability and capacity in a given year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when will repairs be carried out to those parts of the Mount Stewart estate wall that are the responsibility of Forest Service.
answer_text: Forest Service is nearing completion of the clearance of storm damaged trees at Mount Stewart. These ongoing works are expected to be completed in August, following which my officials in Forest Service will commission any necessary work under the terms of the lease in agreement with National Trust.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what measures are being considered to improve traffic flow on the (i) Messines Road; and (ii) Comber Road, Newtownards.
answer_text: Road safety remains an extremely high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users. My Department is aware that traffic can build up on the Messines Road, Newtownards for short periods at peak times which is consistent with traffic patterns and conditions in many of towns and cities across the North.There are no plans to introduce road improvement schemes at these locations, however I am advised that road markings were previously installed as part of a traffic management scheme on the approach to the Comber Road Roundabout to aid traffic flow and supplementary road signage was erected to aid driver discipline.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when NI Water will be carrying out its planned pipelaying works on the Mountstewart Road, Newtownards.
answer_text: NI Water has advised the planned pipelaying works on the Mountstewart Road, Newtownards is scheduled to take place in the next price control (PC 28) which runs from 2028-2033.   For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com . Making direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will introduce legislation to grant local planning authorities the power to revoke Tree Preservation Orders, previously granted by the former Department of Environment Planning Service.
answer_text: The issue of granting councils the power to revoke Tree Preservation Orders (TPOs) was considered in the first report of the Review of the Implementation of the Planning Act (RIPA). In that report, my Department committed to considering legislative change to address this matter.Such a change would require primary legislation. While I have no current plans to amend the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 at this time, the issue remains under active review in line with the Department’s commitment in the RIPA report.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current number of people in receipt of Universal Credit, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The number of people in receipt of Universal Credit, broken down by constituency, is available in table 5b of the supplementary tables from the Universal Credit Publication: Data to August 2024. This publication can be accessed here: https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/articles/universal-credit-statistics#toc-0. question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of roles across the Civil Service that are mainly or exclusively focused on issues of equality, diversity, or inclusivity, expressed in numbers of full-time employees.
answer_text: ANSWERThere are four full time equivalent roles in my Department which are mainly or exclusively focused on issues of equality, diversity or inclusivity. Three of these roles focus on equality, diversity and inclusion for the whole of Civil Service and its circa 24,000 staff.

answer_text: I refer the Member to my response to AQW 28534/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail how many individuals have received a one-off payment through the Diffuse Mesothelioma Payment scheme, since its inception.
answer_text: My Department does not hold the information requested. 		The Diffuse Mesothelioma Payment Scheme is a UK-wide scheme which sits under the responsibility of the Department for Work and Pensions in Great Britain. My Department has no role in relation to this scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 27906/22-27, to detail an assessment of why there has been a significant drop in the amount of fines issued to parents or guardians for non-attendance of children at school since the 2019/20 year.
answer_text: In addition to the impact of the Covid 19 pandemic, the significant period of Action Short of Strike by the Education Authority (EA) Education Welfare Services will have had a significant impact on the number of fines issued.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any further consideration has been given to the publication of a lung health strategy.
answer_text: Rather than launching a lengthy process associated with any new health strategy, I asked officials in my Department and the Public Health Agency to continue their efforts in the current 4 areas of improving services for adult asthma, interstitial lung disease, children’s specialist respiratory care and primary care and diagnostics. I do however recognise the need to formalise these work streams into a plan for Northern Ireland, that builds on these priorities and extends into other areas such as COPD, oxygen therapy and others in 2025/26 and beyond. I have asked my officials to develop this plan for discussion.  This plan will be formalised with a reporting structure, lead officers and clinicians, timescales and milestones for transparent monitoring.The lead commissioner in my Department will be responsible for developing the care pathways developed by the workstreams into commissioning intentions for funding in the future, whilst also implementing system changes that can be achieved without additional investment. These issues, are in need and indeed deserve investment, but like all health care opportunities must be evaluated in line with other emergent pressures.

answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration that has been given to lighting up the exterior of Scrabo Tower.
answer_text: In 2017 HED conducted a trial at Scrabo Tower to assess the viability of lighting the tower. It was concluded that routine lighting was not feasible at the time.  There are no plans at the present to install floodlighting at Scrabo.
