question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the effectiveness of the joint protocol for missing children between his Department and the PSNI since its launch.
answer_text: The  Interface Protocol between HSC Trusts and PSNI (Where a child is reported missing and other police interactions with residential children’s homes) 2023 is due to be formally launched in June 2024, although in the interim period HSC Trusts are already beginning to implement the protocol Currently awareness events are being delivered across PSNI and HSC Trusts and other key stakeholders.  I am assured that after the formal launch in June 2024 robust governance arrangements will be in place to effectively evaluate the impact of the Protocol going forward. The Governance arrangements have been agreed and will in turn inform future review and planning in this important area.The joint ambition underpinning the Protocol, is to create the necessary structures and processes to deliver a strengthened ability to safeguard and protect young people who go missing, or who are vulnerable to same.Current governance structures include the Operational Missing Group (Trust Level) comprising of key operational Trust leads and PSNI.  These Groups facilitate liaison and joint planning between the agencies.  Initial feedback from these Groups are, that this structure is already facilitating disruption of patterns relating to children who repeatedly go missing. The overall aim of the Protocol is to ensure that all children receive an appropriate and timely response, which is competent, compassionate, and consistent, when they are not where they are expected to be; and that everything possible is done by all multi agency partners to reduce risk and harm, to enable a child’s safe return, and prevent repeat missing occurrences.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what representation he has made to the Home Secretary in relation to the need for extended workers' visas in support of the mushroom growing industry.
answer_text: I wrote to the Home Secretary on 8th March 2024 to raise concerns regarding changes to the immigration system and highlight my interest in the Migration Advisory Committee’s inquiry of the Seasonal Worker Visa, which the mushroom industry utilises. I will be meeting with industry representatives to discuss this issue.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will bring forward an ADHD strategy as a matter of urgency.
answer_text: Provision of service for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) assessment and diagnosis has grown organically across Trusts in response to population demand and available expertise within Health & Social Care teams. Whilst a level of provision is available for ADHD referrals there is currently no specifically commissioned services. The Department is aware that this has led to inconsistencies in provision between Trusts and inconsistent pathways and service gaps. The Department of Health Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) are committed to work closely and collaboratively with Health and Social Care Trusts to develop innovative, cost effective, co-produced, quality, and evidenced based services that can meet NICE compliant pathways for ADHD diagnosis and support.DoH SPPG, alongside DoH policy colleagues, are considering how a future ADHD service might be delivered to meet needs, recognising that the demand for ADHD diagnosis has risen in recent years.  In line with the usual process for commissioning services, any decision to commission ADHD services in the future will be based on an assessment of the level of demand for services, and in the context of future budget availability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will make a determination on the Hightown Incinerator.
answer_text: My officials are continuing to progress the application in line with planning policy to a point where a decision is ready to be made.  A number of consultation responses are awaited and, when received and all statutory processes are satisfactorily completed, a recommendation will be brought forward to me by my officials; however, at this time I cannot confirm when a recommendation will be made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many individuals have availed of the Regional Trauma Network through (i) the Victims and Survivors Service; and (ii) Health and Social Care Trust services, for each year of its existence.
answer_text: The Regional Trauma Network (RTN) incorporates a wide range of support and services for victims and survivors both in the statutory sector, and in the community & voluntary (C&V) sector.  The RTN enables a co-ordinated approach to supporting individuals with the right care, at the right time, in the right place – and this can involve Health and Social Care Trusts and C&V organisations working together to meet assessed needs.The Regional Trauma Network (RTN) has been fully operational since the beginning of the 2023/24 financial year. In respect of that year (i) Circa 9,000 individuals have availed of services within the RTN through the Victims and Survivors Service and its 47 community and voluntary partner organisations, and (ii) Circa 600 individuals have availed of services through the Health and Social Care Trust RTN Teams.   Explanatory NoteVictims and Survivors Service (VSS):The VSS delivers health and wellbeing support and services in partnership with 47 community and voluntary organisations across NI.  Areas of support include Talking Therapies, Complementary Therapies, Social Support and other support tailored to the needs of the individual.  Many of the 9,000 individuals supported will avail of support across more than one area.(ii) RTN Trust Trauma Teams:The RTN Teams deliver tailored, evidence-based therapies to individuals as well as group therapies and social support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will meet with representatives of Downpatrick and County Down Railway to discuss ways in which his Department can facilitate and support the heritage railway, including on safety matters.
answer_text: While the Department for Infrastructure is responsible for the regulation of railways in the North including heritage railways, it does not have a remit for funding heritage railways.My officials within Rail Safety Branch are in ongoing contact with Downpatrick and County Down Railway, and given the technical nature of rail safety, they remain available to meet to explore any practical proposals for support as part of their regulatory role.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will bring forward legislation for the mandatory microchipping of cats.
answer_text: My Department’s Code of Practice for the Welfare of Cats already recommends that owners ensure their cats are identified, using either a collar or microchip. However, I am aware that from 10 June 2024, the microchipping of pet cats in England will become mandatory.   	I am considering the mandatory microchipping of pet cats in the context of all the pressing needs for animal welfare reform in Northern Ireland.  However, ultimately, my capacity to enact legislation will be constrained by the resources available and the time left in this shortened mandate.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he intends to publish the Marine Plan for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I intend to publish the adopted Marine Plan for Northern Ireland by the end of 2024. Adoption and publication will be subject to agreement by the Northern Ireland Executive and Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of full-time specialist lecturers; and (ii) the number of temporary, substitute or agency lecturers, employed by the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise, broken down by (a) Enniskillen campus; (b) Loughry campus; and (c) Greenmount campus.
answer_text: The number of full-time Lecturers and the number of temporary, substitute or agency Lecturers at the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise’s Enniskillen, Greenmount and Loughry campuses are provided in the table below.The number of full-time lecturersThe number of temporary, substitute or agency lecturers  Loughry Campus184Enniskillen Campus92Greenmount Campus3210
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will introduce legislation to implement and commence the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 in full.
answer_text: I am currently considering the introduction of five pieces of subordinate legislation that will further implement and commence the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015.  It should be noted that this will not fully commence the Act and that further secondary legislation will be required in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the impact a recurrent £321 million annual funding allocation to Northern Ireland Water will have on planning applications.
answer_text: Budgets for future years have not been agreed, and therefore I have not taken decisions on future budget allocations. To provide a basis for future planning and given the financial constraints my Department has faced, NI Water was provided with a planning scenario based on the 2023-24 opening capital budget position.I will continue to raise the need for NI Water to have secure and sustainable funding, with the NI Executive and the British government, to help deliver the best possible service for customers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he intends to bring forward a new Biodiversity Strategy.
answer_text: A Nature Recovery (Biodiversity) Strategy is currently under development, with a view to launching a public consultation later this year. The timescale for publication of a final agreed strategy will be dependent on the outcome of the consultation exercise.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the target dates for each local planning authority in their original Local Development Plan timetables for the adoption of (i) the Plan Strategy; and (ii) the Local Policies Plan.
answer_text: Local Development Plan timetables are live council documents. Whilst the Department must work with councils to agree their timetable, it is their responsibility to keep them under review. The time taken to produce development plans was underestimated in original timetables. Councils have since updated their timescales and continue to review them, submitting revised timetables to my Department for agreement when necessary.The timing of Local Development Plans is primarily a matter for each Council to decide in line with its priorities and the resources available to it. All current timetables can be viewed on council websites.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when a scheme to upgrade the footpath between Old Shore Road and Teal Rocks, Newtownards, will commence.
answer_text: I can advise that the volume of requests for local transport safety measures greatly exceeds our capacity and therefore priorities must be established in this competition for limited resources on an annual basis.  Officials have advised that an assessment of the proposed improvements to the existing footway at this location did not rank sufficiently high enough to be included within the intended programme of local transport safety measures for 23/24 financial year.Budgets for next year have not yet been set and we are continuing to work closely with the Department of Finance. It would be for the Executive to decide final budget allocations. I will consider this scheme along with my other local transport and safety measures when I receive my budget.  Local transport and safety measures for 24/25 will be presented to Council in due course.As advised during the Adjournment Debate on Tuesday 12th March 2024 in the Assembly Chamber, I have asked my maintenance team to inspect the vegetation along this section of footway and take any necessary action  It is planned that any required vegetation clearance would be programmed to align with the forthcoming surfacing works at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what steps are being taken by his Department to address road surface issues on Killinchy Road, Comber, from the roundabout to Carnesure Cottages.
answer_text: Recent major works by a utility company have led to significant deterioration of this section of Killinchy Road, Comber.My officials are currently in discussions with the utility company to seek a more satisfactory reinstatement that will improve the quality of the surface and prolong the life of the road.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for his assessment of the impact on farm businesses which are unable to bring livestock and semen into Northern Ireland as a consequence of the restrictions imposed by the bluetongue quarantine; and (ii) what steps he is taking to compensate those affected.
answer_text: My officials are engaging regularly with key stakeholders to discuss the impacts of the Bluetongue incursion in Great Britian with the most recent meeting taking place Tuesday 19 March 2023. I am very aware of the individual cases that are impacted by these restrictions however the key message from industry is that priority should be given to ensuring that Northern Ireland remains Bluetongue free and that we work together on a collaborative basis to mitigate against any possible incursion of the disease.My Department is not currently considering a compensation scheme for those farm business which have been impacted by the Bluetongue movement restrictions. I would wish to stress that my primary focus is to keep this devasting disease out of Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the target date; and (ii) the adoption date, for each of the local planning authorities for the adoption of (i) the Plan Strategy; and (ii) the Local Policies Plan.
answer_text: Local Development Plan timetables are live council documents. Whilst the Department must work with councils to agree their timetable, it is their responsibility to keep them under review. The time taken to produce development plans was underestimated in original timetables. Councils have since updated their timescales and continue to review them, submitting revised timetables to my Department for agreement when necessary.The timing of Local Development Plans is primarily a matter for each Council to decide in line with its priorities and the resources available to it. All current timetables can be viewed on council websites.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will publish the outcome of the consultation on design considerations for a Renewable Electricity Support Scheme for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer Ms McIlveen to my answer at Oral Question Time on 19 March 2024 (AQO 175/22-27) that:‘My Department is developing a renewable electricity support scheme to incentivise investment, ensure a fair price for electricity produced locally and encourage diversification to support security of supply.Following our recent consultation, officials have been working with consultants to define the optimal scheme structure. The High-Level Design for the scheme will be published this month as part of the Department’s response to the Consultation.The next phase will involve modelling and a financial impact assessment, alongside the establishment of the Legislative Pathway, State Aid approval and Institutional roles and responsibilities necessary for delivery.Stakeholder engagement will be essential as we move forward to deliver a final scheme design, maximising Community Benefit opportunities and ensuring prosperity for all.’

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will bring the report on the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme to the Executive for consideration.
answer_text: I will be working towards securing the agreement of the Executive on the way forward in relation to the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme at the earliest opportunity.A comprehensive report of the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme consultation, indicating how responses were considered in the formulation of policy, will be published following a decision by the Executive on the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department will bring forward proposals to support individual farm businesses wishing to invest in small-scale renewable schemes.
answer_text: The Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 established a target of 80% of electricity consumption to be from renewable sources by 2030. Essential to meeting this target will be the establishment of a support scheme for renewable electricity. Departmental officials are currently working on a scheme design that will incentivise investment in larger scale renewable electricity projects ensuring consumers pay a fair price for electricity produced locally. Options for micro-generation/small scale will continue to be evaluated as part of our net zero ambitions. Independent advice on solar panels, heat pumps and other energy efficiency matters is available from organisations such as the Energy Saving Trust. These technologies have the potential to provide an attractive payback to businesses without grant support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) what support is available; and (ii) what additional support she will introduce, in relation to non-domestic rates payable for renewable energy plants.
answer_text: There are just under 450 renewable energy properties valued for rates purposes, that are currently in receipt of Small Business Rates Relief, at a cost of just over £300,000.Renewable energy plants are not rateable if (a) they are valued as “associated Plant and Machinery”, (b) they are under 50kw and used for electric generation (c) they are under 45kw thermal and used for the production of heat.Renewable energy plant and machinery that is rateable, but is part of a factory that is entitled to industrial distinguishment, will also benefit from industrial distinguishment.It would be for the Department for Economy, as the policy competent Department for Energy and Renewables, to make the case for any proposals for new or additional rate supports in this area. I will consider any such proposal alongside the findings of the recent Revenue Raising consultation and in aligning the strategic policy options for the rating system with Executive priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide a timescale for the commencement of the resurfacing scheme for Belfast Road, Comber.
answer_text: Work has started on the planning and programme of new schemes for 2024/25 financial year but cannot be finalised until budgets are confirmed.Details of the planned list of resurfacing schemes for the 2024/25 financial year in the North Down area will be included in the Department’s Annual Report to Ards and North Down Borough Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) to detail the cost of processing waste illegally dumped from the Republic of Ireland in the last five years; and (ii) whether the Irish government has contributed to that cost.
answer_text: No processing of waste illegally dumped from the Republic of Ireland has been undertaken within the last five years, therefore no costs have been incurred in that regard.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment on whether the increase in the Carrier Bag Levy has reduced the number of bags in circulation, and to what extent.
answer_text: The most recently published official Carrier Bag levy statistics, published on 17 August 2023, show that in 2022/23 (Year 10 of the levy), 65 million bags were dispensed by retailers.		This is the first set of annual validated statistics available following the introduction of the Single Use Carrier Bags Charge (Amendment and Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2022. Under this legislation, retailers must charge a minimum of 25p (the levy) for each new carrier bag supplied.  This applies to bags of all materials with a retail price of £5 or less. 		Comparisons with the number of bags dispensed in previous years is not possible, as the scope of the levy has changed greatly. In previous years the annual statistics only included carrier bags sold by retailers for less than 20 pence.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs how much has been raised through the Carrier Bag Levy in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The table below provides a breakdown on how much revenue has been raised through the Carrier Bag Levy in each of the last five years; official validated statistics are published each year in August. Financial YearCarrier Bag Levy Revenue2022-23£14.4m2021-22£2.7m2020-212019-202018-19£2.2m£4.4m£4.6mTotal£28.3mIt is worth noting the likely impact of the COVID pandemic, when considering the fluctuations in revenue collected during 2020-21 and 2021-22. The significant increase in 2022-23 followed the introduction of the Single Use Carrier Bags Charge (Amendment and Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2022 in April 2022, when the levy was increased from 5p to 25p and applied to a wider range of bags.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of how much waste is illegally dumped in Northern Ireland from the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: Based on assessments carried out in 2019, it is estimated that up to 137,000m3 of historic illegal waste originating from Ireland remains across 5 sites in Northern Ireland. DAERA officials continue to work on options to resolve these remaining sites, including engagement with Defra and Irish Government counterparts.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs how much waste illegally dumped from the Republic of Ireland has been repatriated in the last five years.
answer_text: No illegally dumped waste from the Republic of Ireland has been repatriated within the last five years. DAERA officials continue to work on options to resolve the remaining sites containing illegal waste originating from the Republic of Ireland, including engagement with Defra and Irish Government counterparts.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the roll-out of LED street lighting in the (i) Ards and North Down Borough Council area; and (ii) Newry, Mourne and Down District Council area.
answer_text: I can confirm that as of 20 March 2024;In the North Down and Ards Council area there are a total of 25,907 street lights of which 12,450 (48%) have been upgraded to LED lanterns.In the Newry Mourne and Down Council area there are a total of 24,310 street lights of which 18,582 (76%) have been upgraded to LED lanterns.

answer_text: Information on spend on youth services is held by district council area. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current priority list of traffic calming measures which are under consideration in the Ards and North Down Borough Council area.
answer_text: Traffic calming schemes are funded through my Department’s Local Transport Safety Measures (LTSM) budget which covers a range of measures aimed at providing a safer road transport system.  Regrettably, the available investment has not been sufficient to deliver all schemes that have been assessed as worthwhile.Whilst a priority list of traffic calming measures in the Ards and North Down Borough Council area has previously been prepared, this list is currently being updated to take account of a number of factors which have changed at the proposed sites, including collision history, traffic speeds, and additional development.Details of the planned list of local transport and safety measures for the 2023/24 financial year are provided in the Department’s Annual Report for Ards and North Down which can be found at; https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/infrastructure/annual-report-ards-and-north-down-borough-council-2023.pdf       The planned programme of work for the 2024/25 year will be finalised when budgets are confirmed and the programme will be presented to Council in due course.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as inconsiderate parking and/or speeding should be directed to the PSNI using the non emergency 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, what low carbon schemes are being provided by the Housing Executive in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for The Northern Ireland Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:       “The Housing Executive has developed an innovative Low Carbon Retrofit Programme to help inform its future heating policy and thermal improvement programme.  The programme comprises some 300 dwellings in a small number of schemes across Northern Ireland, and is aimed at reducing carbon emissions and householder bills and providing healthier homes through the combination of the following interventions:Improved energy efficiency measures.Low carbon heating options, principally air source heat pumps   (ASHP), with options for biomethane and hybrid.Improved ‘time of use’ electricity tariff options.Improved householder education to effect behaviour change.Renewable energy for power generation and electric storage.Two of the schemes in the programme are in the Strangford constituency, in Bowtown estate in Newtownards.  Phase 1 is on site and comprises 22 dwellings, while Phase 2 is planned for 40 dwellings and will commence on site later this year.”             For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, what efforts have been taken to source funding to restore the Martin Memorial Clock in Shrigley, Co. Down.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“It remains the Housing Executive’s position that we are not able to fund the restoration of the memorial from rental income and are unlikely to be able to do so in the foreseeable future.  This is due to the ongoing significant shortfall in the funding that we require to address the maintenance and improvement of our housing stock.Historic Environment Division in the Department for Communities confirmed that as a public body the Housing Executive is not eligible for its grant funding.  Therefore, we have previously attempted to generate partnership arrangements with local stakeholders to create a vehicle - such as a trust - that may be able to secure grant funding, but these were unsuccessful.  Consequently, we have explored a number of alternative funding sources.  In 2022 we submitted an application for funding to Round 2 of the national Levelling Up Fund but unfortunately were unsuccessful.  Last year we had an initial meeting with the Programmes Unit in Newry, Mourne and Down District Council to discuss whether an application for funding could be made to PEACEPLUS under the Rural Regeneration theme or the council’s PEACEPLUS Action Plan.  Following this we contacted the Shrigley 200 committee and other local interested parties with a view to collaborating on a potential application and exploring other funding sources.  However, there was no response and we are now following up on this. Until such times as funding for restoration can be made available or secured we will continue to de-weed the memorial and maintain it in a safe condition.  The Sir Hans Sloane Centre has been particularly helpful to our efforts by taking the bell from the tower and putting it on public display in the centre.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, how many social housing applicants with a disability have been identified as in need of accessible housing in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The request asks for how many social housing applicants with a disability have been identified as in need of accessible housing in the Strangford constituency; this is not specifically recorded on an application. However, the tables below give information on those applicants who are Wheelchair Users, applicants who are deemed to Require Ground Floor (GF) accommodation and those applicants who have Complex Needs in the Strangford parliamentary constituency as at end of December 2023:Parl ConstApplicants - Wheelchair UsersStrangford56Belfast South/Strangford<10Parl ConstApplicants - Requiring Ground Floor Accommodation Strangford391Belfast South/Strangford11Parl ConstApplicants - Complex NeedsStrangford501Belfast South/Strangford23Please note that where there are less than 10 applicants or allocations, they have been listed as <10 in compliance with Data Protection requirements.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following comments by the Speaker at the conclusion of Question Time on 12 March 2024, to detail the criteria for taking part in the heat pump schemes being delivered by the Housing Executive.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Given that Ms McIlveen has also submitted AQW 8990/22-27 regarding NIHE’s 300 Low Carbon Programme (that includes the installation of heat pumps), NIHE have assumed that this query is about the criteria for properties’ inclusion in this pilot programme.In summary the locations/ properties were selected with reference to the following criteria:The properties should:Have had recent External Cyclical Maintenance works carried out to ensure the integrity of their external fabricHave double glazing to current Housing Executive standardsBe due replacement of their heating systemsTwo projects were identified in each of the Housing Executive’s operational Regional areas in order to ensure that:Each Heating Contractor had at least one scheme, to facilitate learning for their operatives.Each Housing Executive Regional Heating Services Unit had involvement for training purposes on installation and maintenance.A variety of heating types were included, and alternative options considered.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will bring forward legislation to amend the Defective Premises (Northern Ireland) Order 1975 to provide a remedy similar to that under Section 135 of the Building Safety Act 2022.
answer_text: The Minister for Communities has confirmed that his department is the lead policy department with respect to building safety in Northern Ireland and has accepted the transfer of the 1975 Order to his department by way of a Transfer of Functions Order which my officials are currently progressing.	It will be for DfC officials to explore the way forward in relation to any changes in the limitation periods in Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, following delays in the Forestry Expansion Scheme and Small Woodland Grant Scheme, what steps his Department is taking to ensure approvals for new woodland creation are facilitated for the 2023-24 planting season.
answer_text: Approvals for forestry grant applications are being taken forward as a high priority both by Forest Service and the Northern Ireland Environment Agency.The necessary statutory assessments for the applications have been substantially completed and work is well advanced on those remaining. The next stage is to secure financial approval of the business case, to allow letters of offer to issue and planting to get underway and I am hopeful that this step can be completed very shortly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether revitalisation funding can be made available following the public realm scheme in Portaferry.
answer_text: My Department has a regeneration policy focus on urban settlements with a population of 5,000 or more people. However, I understand the challenges faced by our rural communities, so I am pleased that my officials are continuing to work closely with colleagues in the Ards and North Down Borough Council to deliver the £2m Portaferry Public Realm project, funded through the Covid Recovery Small Settlements Programme. This is a bespoke collaborative approach between my Department, the Department for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs and the Department for Infrastructure to deliver a programme of projects to support smaller settlements as they recover from the impact of the pandemic and to address regeneration needs.As Portaferry has a population below the 5,000 threshold, it will not be possible for my Department to provide funding for a revitalisation project at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether consideration is being given for both ferries to operate at the same time between Portaferry and Strangford during peak times and holiday periods.
answer_text: Strangford Lough Ferry Service has provided additional sailings using both ferries on many occasions. When the current industrial action, short of strike action has ended my officials will consider operating both vessels again.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether consideration has been given to an early morning Strangford ferry service.
answer_text: Strangford Lough Ferry Service has provided additional early morning sailings on many occasions. When the current industrial action, short of strike action has ended, my officials will consider a return to additional early morning sailings.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the proposed dropped kerbs scheme will commence in the Beverley development, Newtownards.
answer_text: My Department takes the needs of pedestrians very seriously, especially those with mobility difficulties.  We have invested considerable resources in both improving existing and, where possible, providing new facilities for pedestrians where this can be achieved within our limited resources.  As part of this work, my Department provides dropped kerbs and tactile paving along strategic routes and in areas of high pedestrian activity.My officials will assess Beverley development, Newtownards for potential dropped kerb locations within the next number of months.Given the limited budgets provided to my Department, the work programme for this type of work is heavily oversubscribed.  However, any locations identified will be considered for inclusion in the work programme for the next financial year along with other competing dropped kerb requests at other locations within the area.Following consideration of my priorities and confirmation of budgets, the work programme for 2024/25 will be prepared and presented to Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the dropped kerbs in Stratheden Heights, Newtownards, will be installed.
answer_text: My Department take the needs of pedestrians very seriously, especially those with mobility difficulties.  We have invested considerable resources in both improving existing and, where possible, providing new facilities for pedestrians where this can be achieved within our limited resources.  As part of this work, my Department provides dropped kerbs and tactile paving along strategic routes and in areas of high pedestrian activity.Following an assessment by my Officials, a dropped kerb design plan has been created for Stratheden Heights, from Movilla Road to Lawnbrook Drive and 12 no. locations have been identified for works. Given the limited budgets provided to my Department, the work programme for this type of work is heavily oversubscribed.  However, this site will be considered for inclusion in the work programme for the next financial year along with other competing dropped kerb requests at other locations within the area.Following consideration of my priorities and confirmation of budgets, the work programme for 2024/25 will be prepared and presented to Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his Department's oversight role in relation to (i) corporate plans; (ii) service and action plans; and (iii) measures of performance, of bodies sponsored by it.
answer_text: My Department works collaboratively with our Arm’s Length Bodies (ALBs) in the development of their corporate and business plans ensuring plans align with Departmental strategy. Performance is monitored through Key Performance Indicators and regular accountability meetings.There is also collaborative working on business developments aligned with policy objectives.Management and financial information is shared over the course of a year and ALBs provide bi-annual assurance statements to the Department as part of the Department’s overall assurance arrangements.Oversight and engagement arrangements are set out in Management Statements and Financial Memorandum (MSFMs) or Partnership Agreements with ALBs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will introduce legislation regarding the regulation of cosmetic treatments such as botox.
answer_text: My Department continues to take decisive action to combat illegal promotion, supply or misuse of medicines and to alerting the public about the dangers of using unauthorised medicines outside of the regulated supply chain. Under the Independent Health Care Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005, the Regulation and Quality Improvement Authority (RQIA) regulate clinics providing certain laser or intense pulsed light cosmetic treatments and providers are subject to certain requirements for example to undergo appropriate training.The risk to public safety posed by the unlawful distribution and misuse of unauthorised medicinal products within the non-surgical cosmetic sector in Northern Ireland is a serious issue. Such breaches relating to Northern Ireland may be referred to my Department’s Medicines Regulatory Group (MRG) for investigation under the Human Medicines Regulations, 2012.MRG works to ensure continuing compliance with existing laws and guidance and where evidence suggests unlawful practice is occurring, enforcement actions are taken. Where a breach of the Regulations is identified MRG will consider a range of enforcement options proportionate to the circumstances. This includes securing compliance with the Regulations through the issuing of warning or advice, referral to professional bodies or prosecution.My Department remains vigilant and continues to work with key partner agencies to monitor the illicit marketplace and will continue to take effective action where necessary to protect public safety in Northern Ireland.My Department is aware of the wider, changing health and social care service delivery environment, including in relation to cosmetic treatments. Prior to the COVID-19 pandemic, the Department had developed a new draft regulatory policy framework however further development work is required, including consultation on the draft policy.  The Department is currently operating within a constrained budget and is required to make decisions in relation to the work that can be delivered within current resources. In that context, work on the review of the regulation is currently paused to allow for other priority projects to progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plan to improve the electric vehicle infrastructure in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: The provision and operation of publicly accessible electric vehicle charge points is a commercial operation in the North of Ireland. Responsibility for the maintenance and installation of any commercially operated charge point rests solely with the charge point operator. Current providers operating in the North include ESB, Weev, Maxol, BP Pulse and Easygo. The market is also open to other commercial operators who would wish to provide charging infrastructure.The relevant charge point operators are best placed to advise you of their plans for the installation of charge points in your constituency.Funding opportunities for the installation of charge point infrastructure are also provided by the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV). These schemes are open to Councils who provide charging infrastructure for EV owners, or potential owners, who do not have driveway access (On Street Residential Charge Point Scheme – ORCS), and for residential and commercial landlords. My Department has made match-funding available to assist local councils making an application to ORCS.Derry City and Strabane District Council made an application under the 2021-22 round of funding on behalf of nine Local Councils. Newry, Mourne & Down, Lisburn & Castlereagh & Ards & North Down councils are all part of the consortium. This application was successful, and the consortium received a Grant Offer Letter from OZEV in July 2022 for funding to install 124 charge points across these council areas. Derry City and Strabane District Council would be best placed to provide you with an update on their plans for the Strangford Constituency.My Department has also set up an EV Infrastructure Task-Force, bringing together representatives from government, consumers, energy providers, industry and EV drivers to consider the barriers to enhancing our EV charging infrastructure requirements.  An Action Plan to deliver a commercially led, fit for purpose, modern EV charging network was agreed in autumn 2022. The Action Plan can be found here:https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/electric-vehicle-ev-infrastructure-action-plan .

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether the policies on the categories of properties currently benefiting from rates relief are to be reviewed.
answer_text: I am committed to ensuring we continue to develop the rating system to ensure it is fair, equitable and progressive and that it is aligned with the Executive’s economic vision, expanding our taxbase and supporting businesses to grow and create jobs.Alongside the findings of the recent Revenue Raising consultation, I will be assessing strategic policy options within the rating system, including the current rate reliefs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether an assessment has been carried out in relation to the requirement of parents to pay a voluntary contribution in certain schools.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 7474/22-27. The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 1 March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will encourage the Electoral Office to find alternative venues for polling stations, to avoid days being lost to the education of pupils in schools being used as polling stations.
answer_text: ANSWERThe Department has previously engaged with both the Electoral Office and the Northern Ireland Office on this issue. My officials have consistently emphasised the need to the Electoral Office to consider using venues other than schools and to consider conducting elections at weekends. The current legislative position allows for the Electoral Office to utilise school premises and that schools cannot refuse.  In the most recent correspondence  on this matter, my Department was advised that the Electoral Office were “not aware of a likelihood of the opening of discussion to alter the statutory arrangements.”

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether the domestic rates cap is to be removed or raised.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the reply I gave to AQW 8430/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to clarify the original anticipated timescale for development until implementation of a local development plan, when powers were initially passed to local councils.
answer_text: There is no legislative timeframe for Councils to bring forward their LDPs. While a diagram within the Strategic Planning Policy Statement (SPPS), suggested 40 months to bring forward both a Plan Strategy and a Local Policies Plan this was produced for indicative purposes only and not as a target.Prior to publishing their LDP, each council must take the time to undertake rigorous surveying of their area to develop a robust evidence base upon which to build their emerging planning policies. This will vary from council to council.I am determined that we need to build further upon the progress made to date in relation to progressing council LDPs, and through the Planning improvement Programme stakeholders including my Department, Councils and statutory consultees are reviewing the current processes so the plan system can be more responsive in our drive to support sustainable development for our businesses and communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has undertaken a review of planning fees including (i) an automatic annual inflationary uplift; and (ii) multiple fees for retrospective applications as part of a wider review of planning fees.
answer_text: The report on the Review of the Implementation of the Planning Act (NI) 2011 made numerous recommendations including the implementation of a planning fee inflationary uplift which took effect in April 2023.  Following my decision, my officials have also recently informed the Committee for Infrastructure of a further inflationary uplift.  This will take effect in the near future, and when the Committee is content with the proposal for the Statutory Rule.Early work has also commenced, between my officials and local government, on scoping the challenges and opportunities around securing the long-term financial sustainability of the planning service/function at local government level in the two-tier planning system.  Progress is however dependent on the resources available to me within my Department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the assessments for applications under the Central Good Relations Fund were completed.
answer_text: Applications to the 2024/25 Central Good Relations Fund are currently being assessed. This process will be completed in April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the availability of NHS dental services for new patients in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: General Dental Practitioners are independent contractors and are not required to register new patients. Neither does the Department commission Health Service places in practices, for adults or children.There are thirteen Practices in the Strangford Assembly Area who have made arrangements with the Strategic Performance and Planning Group to provide Health Service dentistry. The Department does not hold any information as to whether these practices currently have room to accept new registrations of children.General Dental Practices in the Strangford Assembly Area at 31st December 2023Practice Name1,2Practice AddressArdent Dental Care14 PORTAFERRY ROAD, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 8NNArds Dental Practice16 REGENT STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4LHBelmont Orthodontics10 FRANCES STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4JACastlebawn Dental Practice63 SOUTH STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4JUComber Dental Practice18 KILLINCHY STREET, COMBER, BT23 5APKircubbin Dental68-70 MAIN STREET, KIRCUBBIN, BT22 2SPLawson Dental56 CASTLE STREET, COMBER, BT23 5DZLawson Dental11 MAIN STREET, BALLYNAHINCH, BT24 8DNMontalto Dental Care22 MAIN STREET, BALLYNAHINCH, BT24 8DNParks Dental Care19 FRANCES STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 7DWSaintfield Dental Care16-18 MAIN STREET, SAINTFIELD, BT24 7AAVita Dental Care5-7 CASTLE STREET, COMBER, BT23 5DYXquisite Dental5 NORTH STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4DEActive dental surgeries at the 31st December 2023. question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the successful applicants under the Central Good Relations Fund will be notified.
answer_text: All applicants will be updated on the status of their application once a budget allocation to the Fund is confirmed for 2024/25.

answer_text: General Dental Practitioners are independent contractors and are not required to register new patients. Neither does the Department commission Health Service places in practices, for adults or children.There are thirteen Practices in the Strangford Assembly Area who have made arrangements with the Strategic Performance and Planning Group to provide Health Service dentistry. The Department does not hold any information as to whether these practices currently have room to accept new registrations of children.General Dental Practices in the Strangford Assembly Area at 31st December 2023Practice Name1,2Practice AddressArdent Dental Care14 PORTAFERRY ROAD, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 8NNArds Dental Practice16 REGENT STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4LHBelmont Orthodontics10 FRANCES STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4JACastlebawn Dental Practice63 SOUTH STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4JUComber Dental Practice18 KILLINCHY STREET, COMBER, BT23 5APKircubbin Dental68-70 MAIN STREET, KIRCUBBIN, BT22 2SPLawson Dental56 CASTLE STREET, COMBER, BT23 5DZLawson Dental11 MAIN STREET, BALLYNAHINCH, BT24 8DNMontalto Dental Care22 MAIN STREET, BALLYNAHINCH, BT24 8DNParks Dental Care19 FRANCES STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 7DWSaintfield Dental Care16-18 MAIN STREET, SAINTFIELD, BT24 7AAVita Dental Care5-7 CASTLE STREET, COMBER, BT23 5DYXquisite Dental5 NORTH STREET, NEWTOWNARDS, BT23 4DEActive dental surgeries at the 31st December 2023. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will bring forward proposals to supplement existing provisions in Section 59 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, which would disallow the variation of a development proposal at appeal.
answer_text: Following consideration of the available evidence, the report on the Review of the Implementation of the Planning Act (NI) 2011 (RIPA) made numerous recommendations/actions covering key elements of the planning system. Good progress has already been made to date, including the implementation of a planning fee inflationary uplift; the progression of work to bring forward arrangements for statutory validation checklists which will improve the quality of applications entering the system; the launch (December 2023) of a public consultation into a fundamental review of the Planning (Development Management) Regulations (NI) 2015; and a review of the internal administration of the ‘call-in’ process. Enhanced use of digital technology in the planning system has also been aided with introduction of the Regional Planning IT System (Planning Portal). The report gave a commitment to review aspects of the planning appeals system around potentially restricting the variation of development proposals at appeal. I will continue to monitor progress with my officials on the recommendations in the report, and other proposals aimed at improving the planning system. Progress is however dependent on the resources available to me within my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he has considered introducing fixed penalty notices to advertisement control.
answer_text: Following consideration of the available evidence, the report on the Review of the Implementation of the Planning Act (NI) 2011 (RIPA) made numerous recommendations/actions covering key elements of the planning system. Good progress has already been made to date, including the implementation of a planning fee inflationary uplift; the progression of work to bring forward arrangements for statutory validation checklists which will improve the quality of applications entering the system; the launch (December 2023) of a public consultation into a fundamental review of the Planning (Development Management) Regulations (NI) 2015; and a review of the internal administration of the ‘notification’ process. Enhanced use of digital technology in the planning system has also been aided with introduction of the Regional Planning IT System (Planning Portal). The report gave a commitment that the Department will explore the possibility of applying Fixed Penalty Notices to advertisement control, acknowledging however that such changes, if taken forward, will require amendment to primary and/or subordinate legislation.  I will be reviewing progress with my officials on this, and other proposals aimed at improving the planning system; progress is however dependent on the resources available to me within my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what assessment has been carried out of the key skills and experience gap across the planning system.
answer_text: I recognise that training is an important component of improving the planning process and my Department has worked closely with both the Northern Ireland Local Government Association (NILGA) which has established training programmes for elected representatives and also with planning officers within council planning authorities.The identification of training needs and experience gaps for both planning officers and elected representatives across local government is primarily the responsibility of each council. However, my Department will continue to work collaboratively with NILGA and councils to address any strategic issues in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration his Department has given to appointing independent examiners under Section 10 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 to supplement the work of the Planning Appeals Commission in addressing the Local Development Plan backlog.
answer_text: I recognise the resourcing challenges of the Planning Appeals Commission and acknowledge that, under the Planning Act (NI) 2011, the Department of Infrastructure has powers to appoint independent persons to conduct certain matters such as examinations under Section 10 instead of the Commission. However, this is not necessarily a simple undertaking, and I will be considering this issue with my officials in the coming weeks. Also, my officials have been engaging with them to encourage the best use of existing resources and procedures and overall, I want to work with all partners to ensure that everyone is playing their part to support and deliver an efficient and effective planning system, although that is subject to all parts of the planning system being properly funded and resourced, including the Planning Appeals Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether a review has been carried out of the statutory list of consultees in plan-making to determine whether the current list remains relevant and appropriate to local planning authorities.
answer_text: As part of the Planning Improvement Programme, and a wider review of Local Development Plan Regulations 2015, my officials are currently examining feedback from Local Planning Authorities on the role and function of consultation bodies, including statutory consultees in the Local Development Plan process and will provide me with briefing on this in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what consultation is being planned before he brings forward legislation on measures to control ownership of XL Bully dogs.
answer_text: I plan to establish a working group containing representatives from councils, in their capacity as the relevant enforcement bodies, to assist in the design and implementation of control measures.There is no legal requirement for me to launch a public consultation and I am satisfied that making legislation as soon as possible is the most appropriate way to protect public safety.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what plans there are for O’Neill Memorial Primary School building, Crossnacreevy.
answer_text: The Education Authority is currently exploring whether the site and buildings could be redeveloped for an alternative educational use, subject to sufficient funding.  This is in response to the forecast increase in demand for places in special schools and other specialist provisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what impact his announcement on not extending the Local Government (Coronavirus) (Flexibility of District Council Meetings) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2020 will have on other bodies, such as ARC21 and Libraries NI board meetings.
answer_text: The decision not to extend the Local Government (Coronavirus) (Flexibility of District Council Meetings) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2020 will mean that any flexibility provided by these Regulations to council meetings, which includes the meetings of an executive of a council,	a joint committee of two or more councils (such as arc21) and any committee or sub-committee of those mentioned, has ceased.Any impact will be short term as I intend to bring forward, in as quick a timeframe as possible, permanent provision to provide councils with the powers to hold remote/hybrid meetings using enable powers in the Local Government (Meetings and Performance) Act (NI) 2021.  I think this is a more appropriate solution as I do not believe there is justification in prolonging provisions made under the Coronavirus Act as this was intended as emergency legislation.  The Local Government (Coronavirus) (Flexibility of District Council Meetings) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2020 did not apply to Libraries NI which operates under the Libraries Act (NI) 2008.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will provide additional resources to the Planning Appeals Commission to address increased pressures from the current and anticipated workloads, particularly in relation to the independent examination of local development plans.
answer_text: The Planning Appeals Commission is an independent appellant body. The Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) has sponsorship responsibility for the Commission, and I am advised that officials continue to meet with the Chief Commissioner to address resourcing pressures with work on-going both in terms of recruitment exercises and the future resourcing model, which will be subject to budget constraints.I can confirm that additional expenditure has been provided to the Commission from late 2020 to support the delivery of work associated with Independent Examinations of Local Development Plans for each council district.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what consultation took place prior to his statement to the Assembly on 5 March 2024 on measures to control ownership of XL Bully dogs.
answer_text: I met with the USPCA, and my officials met with members of the Dog Control Coalition and the Northern Ireland Companion Animal Welfare Group.My officials also engaged with the Northern Ireland Dog Advisory Group who represent our local councils’ dog warden services.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of Housing Executive properties currently listed as uninhabitable; (ii) the total projected cost to bring them into a habitable state; and (iii) how many are beyond a habitable state of repair, in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“For the purposes of this Assembly Question, uninhabitable properties are defined as those that cannot currently be occupied as a result of:Fire damage;vandalism;other exceptional reasons e.g. properties which require revision of the internal layout to facilitate letting of what were formerly community group lets. One property is listed as uninhabitable at present in the Strangford constituency. The table below provides a breakdown of the property involved. This property is not beyond a habitable state of repair, subject to the necessary work being undertaken at the estimated cost below:ConstituencyNo of PropertiesEstimated Total Cost to bring to habitable stateStrangford1£85k” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide an assessment of the inspection and maintenance programme for vehicle lifts in MOT centres.
answer_text: The DVA has a robust inspection and planned preventative maintenance programme in place for vehicle testing equipment, including vehicle lifts.The equipment provider’s recommendation is for an annual inspection, however DVA has minor inspections carried out on an eight weekly basis and major inspections on a six-monthly basis. The inspection frequency for some vehicle lifts has recently been increased from an eight-weekly inspection to a four weekly inspection following the identification of small hairline cracks in the welds of the main scissor bushing. The DVA also has independent insurance inspectors who carry out thorough examinations of the lifts in line with health & safety, and legislative requirements.  Such inspections are carried out on a rolling six-monthly basis and any issues arising are addressed by the equipment provider.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how many times the Strangford ferry has been suspended or cancelled due to (i) adverse weather conditions; and (ii) maintenance issues, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: There are 22,500 scheduled sailings of the Strangford ferry each year.Adverse weather occurrences take into consideration, fog, storm, high tides, and the likely events over which DfI have no control, and may impact more than one sailing.YearAdverse Weather Occurrences                     Number of missed sailings2019139320207222021161272022310202319126Maintenance occurrences take into consideration, maintenance issues, technical issues, fuel issues, and vessel swapping.YearMaintenance Issue Occurrences                     Number of missed sailings201951120202102021623202231720239169

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the criteria used to decide the amount of the overall available budget which is allocated to each local council area for both (a) structural; and (b) routine maintenance of the road network; and (ii) the measures he will take to ensure that allocations are based on need.
answer_text: In distributing the budget available for structural maintenance, allocations relating to the areas covered by my Department’s four Roads Divisions are made on the basis of need using a range of weighted indicators tailored to each maintenance activity (i.e. resurfacing, surface dressing drainage etc.).  These take into consideration criteria such as road length, structural condition, traffic and population (for footway resurfacing).For routine maintenance activities (i.e. patching, grass cutting etc) factors including defect information, areas of grass, numbers of gullies etc are taken into consideration in distributing the resources available. In addition to this, the capacity of my Department’s internal contractor, and any associated requirement to use external contractors, is factored in to ensure that the current ‘Limited Maintenance Service’ is delivered. Divisions will also broadly use these indicators when apportioning across council areas to ensure, as far as possible, an equitable distribution of funds.However, the exact combination of indicators and metrics used within each Roads Division will vary and be tailored to the individual needs and circumstances in that Division at that time. Yearly allocations vary to reflect funding pressures within Section Office areas and are also influenced by specific allocations for high priority works such as Trunk Road Resurfacing schemes and structural stability, including coastal defence repairs. As such, it would not be appropriate for a local Section Office or Council area to receive a fixed percentage of the available allocation each year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) how many schools in the Strangford constituency do not have a 20mph speed limit in the vicinity of their campus; and (ii) how many of these meet the criteria for a 20 mph speed limit.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints. Schools have been assessed in accordance with the Department’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070 The Department’s list of schools assessed under this programme is based on council boundaries.  This information is not held by constituency.  Within the Ards and North Down Council area, there have been 62 schools assessed for part-time 20 mph limits.  Of these schools, 16 have part time 20 installed and 46 currently do not have part-time 20 mph speed limits. The assessment of this programme is based on a scoring system rather than criteria thresholds for inclusion.  The top scoring schools for each tranche were chosen and further assessed for suitability on site through discussion with the PSNI.Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.I have provided links to tranche 1 and 2 which contains all the schools within Southern Division which have been included in this road safety scheme.https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/news/mallon-announces-part-time-20mph-speed-limits-becoming-operational-103-schools https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/news/mallon-announces-further-roll-out-20mph-speed-limit-schemes-106-schools

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will work with Community Pharmacy NI to ensure fair and sustainable funding for pharmacies.
answer_text: I can assure the member that I am committed to working with Community Pharmacy NI (CPNI) to deliver fair, sustainable and affordable funding for community pharmacy. Following an urgent meeting I held with representatives of CPNI on 21st February 2024, I have committed to an inflationary uplift of £6.1m for pharmacy services in 2023/24. Furthermore, an additional £4m was provided in recognition of service pressures for the 2023/24 year. The Business Services Organisation arranged for payment of 10/12 of this (£8.4m) with the February payment. Further work will be taken forward to review community pharmacy contractual arrangements in the coming months with CPNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will engage with his counterpart in the Irish government in relation to the Irish Republic’s proposals to ban bottom trawling in their 0-6 mile zone.
answer_text: The Irish Government has launched a consultation process on a review of trawling activity inside its 0-6 nautical mile zone. I would encourage Northern Ireland stakeholders and interested parties to contribute to the consultation. I intend to engage with my counterpart directly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in relation to the proposed closure of fishing grounds by the Isle of Man government, whether he will be engaging with the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs and his counterpart in the Isle of Man to develop a solution that protects Northern Ireland’s interests.
answer_text: The Isle of Man government has recently launched a stakeholder consultation on the introduction of new spatial management measures in the Western Irish Sea Mud-belt to deliver co-benefits in sustainable fisheries, blue carbon research, and marine conservation within the Isle of Man territorial waters.My officials are reviewing the proposals in relation to their potential impact on the Northern Ireland fleet and will be engaging with the Isle of Man on this matter.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has been in contact with the Home Office in relation to the new salary threshold for a Skilled Workers Visa and the impact this will have on the Northern Ireland fishing industry.
answer_text: I have written to the Home Secretary in relation to the introduction of the increased salary threshold to highlight to him the impact of this policy change for agri-food and fisheries businesses in Northern Ireland who rely on this visa route to address labour shortages. I have requested a meeting with the Home Secretary to discuss.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) any discussions he has had with the Minister of Finance in relation to funding the Fishing and Seafood Development Programme’s capital project recommendations; and (ii) any further discussions planned on this issue.
answer_text: I have not discussed the Fishing and Seafood Development Programme recommendations with the Minister of Finance at this stage.The proposed investment is subject to business case approval, planning approval and granting of the necessary marine licences.  Environmental and technical studies are currently being procured to develop more detailed designs and inform the Outline Business Case for this programme.  I will have discussions with the Minister of Finance and Executive colleagues at the appropriate times, in due course, to obtain agreement to proceed with the harbour investment and secure the appropriate level of funding.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what level of budget for new books does Libraries NI require to meet the public library standard.
answer_text: Libraries NI would need an estimated annual investment of £4.3m for new books to meet the public library standard target.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department remains committed to delivering on the recommendations of the Fishing and Seafood Development Programme.
answer_text: The Fishing and Seafood Development Programme made a series of recommendations for investment in the fishing and seafood sectors. I am committed to the recommendations relating to investment in harbour infrastructure and my Department is in the process of procuring integrated consultant teams to undertake the necessary environmental and technical studies for Ardglass and Kilkeel harbours. This will inform the outline business case, which must be approved by Department of Finance before any decision to proceed with the recommended infrastructure development can be taken.  The Fishing and Seafood Development programme made recommendations on other development needs for the fisheries sector and these will be supported through a marine environment and fisheries fund that I will be launching this year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when allocations will be made under the Central Good Relations Fund.
answer_text: The assessment process for the 2024/25 Central Good Relations Fund (CGRF) is currently underway. Allocations will be made after a budget for the CGRF has been confirmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much Libraries NI spent on new books in each of the last five years as a result of (i) budget allocations; and (ii) in-year reallocations.
answer_text: The amount spent by Libraries NI on new books in each of the last five years is tabled below.
