question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what steps he will take to ensure Libraries NI has a sufficient budget to purchase enough books to meet the public library standard.
answer_text: My officials continue to engage with Libraries NI regarding 2024/25 resource funding requirements, including what would enable Libraries NI to meet the public library standard for book stock.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the redevelopment of Belfast Central Library.
answer_text: An updated Strategic Outline Case for the redevelopment of Belfast Central Library was approved by my Department in October 2022.  I am advised that Libraries NI was unable to progress work on an update of the Outline Business Case for the project during 2023/24 due to funding constraints.  The redevelopment of Belfast Central Library remains a key strategic priority for Libraries NI, and it will seek to progress a review of the Outline Business Case during 2024/25, resources permitting.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he plans to launch the consultation on the draft climate action plan.
answer_text: I am fully committed to delivering a Climate Action Plan that is transformational, evidence-based and informed by stakeholders. Given the cross-cutting nature of the Climate Action Plan, the draft will need to be agreed by the Executive, prior to its publication for the required 16-week consultation. The timeframes involved mean that it will not be possible to meet the dates for final publication as set out in the Climate Change Act. However, I am determined that the draft will be published for consultation as soon as possible.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he plans to bring forward a decommissioning scheme to assist in the modernisation of the Northern Ireland fishing fleet.
answer_text: I have no plans to bring forward a decommissioning scheme for the Northern Ireland Fishing fleet at this time.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether an assessment has been made on the potential impact on the (i) agri-food sector; and (ii) wider Northern Ireland economy of a reduction in livestock numbers to meet Climate Change Act 2008 targets.
answer_text: A preliminary and simplified analysis has been undertaken to illustrate the potential impact of scenarios drawn from the Climate Change Committee’s published advice to the Department of April 2023. My Department will wish to develop a more sophisticated capability to analyse policy options that would take on board likely dynamic adjustments within the economy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether an assessment has been carried out on whether the operational protocol for assessing ammonia in planning applications is hindering the replacement of old buildings with newer and more environmentally-friendly development.
answer_text: There has been no formal assessment of the outcomes for replacement buildings as there is no legal basis for treating replacements differently to new buildings in terms of assessing environmental impacts. Competent authorities are required to assess the potential environmental impact of all projects, irrespective of whether they are for ‘new’ or ‘replacement’ developments.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail all Housing Executive refurbishment schemes planned in the Strangford constituency in the next three years.
answer_text: Refurbishment schemes planned for the Strangford constituency are an operational matter for the Housing Executive. You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in relation to recording children who go missing from residential or foster care, to detail (i) the process; (ii) the date on which the process came into effect; (iii) how many residential care homes have used it; and (iv) how many incidents of missing children have been recorded.
answer_text: The tables below outline the number of reports to HSCB/ Department of Health for children and young people who have been Missing from Care over 24hrs within the last 5 years.  In response to the question the timeframe of 1st April 2018 to 31st March 2023 was utilised (5years).Time Period 1st April 2022 – 31st March 2023Number of untoward event notifications of young people missing >24hrsBelfast Trust 22Northern Trust8South Eastern Trust6Southern Trust15Western Trust19Time Period 1st April 2021 – 31st March 2022Number of untoward event  notifications of young people missing >24hrsBelfast Trust 21Northern Trust<5South Eastern Trust<5Southern Trust15Western Trust15Time Period 1st April 2020 – 31st March 2021Number of untoward event notifications of young people missing >24hrsBelfast Trust 32Northern Trust7South Eastern Trust13Southern Trust5Western Trust5Time Period 1st April 2019 – 31st March 2020Number of untoward event notifications of young people missing >24hrsBelfast Trust 44Northern Trust<5South Eastern Trust11Southern Trust13Western Trust12Time Period 1st April 2018 – 31st March 2019Number of untoward event notifications of young people missing >24hrsBelfast Trust 36Northern Trust28South Eastern Trust10Southern Trust14Western Trust12the process Children and young people who go missing from care are reported to the Department of Health (SPPG, previously the Health and Social Care Board) since 2022 via an ‘Untoward Event Notification’ process.  Untoward Events should be reported within 72 hours, unless earlier notification is indicated.the date on which the process came into effect Guidance for Untoward Event Notifications has been in operation since 2010.  To support the management of children or young people who go missing from care an interagency Regional Protocol was developed with PSNI in 2012 ‘Regional Guidance Police Intervention in Residential Units.  Safeguarding of Children Missing from Home and Foster Care May 2012’ with updated versions in 2015 ‘Missing Children Protocol (Runaway and Missing from Care and Home)’, and in 2023 ‘Interface Protocol between HSC Trusts and PSNI (Where a child is reported missing and other police interactions with residential children’s homes)’.how many residential care homes have used it It is the responsibility of the individual HSC Trust to ensure the reporting of all incidents to the Department of Health (SPPG) directly for Looked After Children for whom they are responsible, regardless of where the child is placed i.e. in a Children’s Home, Foster Care, supporting living complex, living at home etc. Where a young person has a placement in a regional facility such as Lakewood Secure Care Centre, the proforma should be submitted from the Trust who placed the child there. Each Trust has their own internal governance process to ensure appropriate reporting pertaining to the criteria within Untoward Notifiable Events. There is a clear directive that all Children’s Homes follow this

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how the £8.1 million, announced for additional resurfacing schemes to address the poor condition of our road network, will be allocated by DfI (i) Roads Division; and (ii) Roads Section offices.
answer_text: The additional £8.1m funding for my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme is to deliver additional resurfacing schemes and continue to address the poor condition of our road network. This funding has now been allocated across all four of my Department’s road divisions.My Department will allocate funding and prioritise decisions and interventions on the basis of a number of data factors including road usage and general surface condition surveys as well as information from maintenance inspections, public enquiries and public liability claims, along with the professional engineering judgement of local engineers. Work has started on the planning and delivery of new schemes for delivery this financial year. However, the development of the programmes is on-going so a full breakdown of these is not yet available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice (i) whether she will introduce legislation to reverse the convictions of sub-postmasters in Northern Ireland, as a result of the Horizon scandal; and (ii) when such legislation will be tabled.
answer_text: I was deeply disappointed by the UK Government’s announcement on 22 February that the legislation they plan to bring forward to reverse the convictions of sub-postmasters will apply in England and Wales only.Whilst the operation of courts in Northern Ireland is a devolved matter, I firmly believe that sub-postmasters who are affected by unjust convictions should receive swift justice and equitable treatment in line with their counterparts in the rest of the UK.  I remain of the view, therefore, that the Westminster legislation should be UK-wide.  This would be the best way forward, given that postal services are a reserved matter and the associated compensation schemes are UK-wide. This would also be the fastest legislative solution and avoids differential treatment between jurisdictions, including in terms of accessing compensation.  I have written to the Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Business and Trade, Kevin Hollinrake MP, jointly with the First Minister and deputy First Minister, in those terms. I have, in addition, written to the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland asking him to support my position.I have also asked my officials to review what other options may be available to provide a remedy for sub-postmasters in Northern Ireland, in the event that the decision remains that the Westminster legislation will not be extended.  These options include taking forward separate legislation through the Assembly to replicate the Westminster provisions, which would create additional delays, or requiring the individuals affected to appeal their convictions through the courts.Given work is ongoing to identify a way forward, I do not have a timeframe available at present. However, I remain committed to ensuring that a swift and equitable remedy is delivered to Northern Ireland sub-postmasters as soon as possible.

answer_text: The tables below outline the number of reports to HSCB/ Department of Health for children and young people who have been Missing from Care over 24hrs within the last 5 years. To support the management of children or young people who go missing from care an interagency Regional Protocol was developed with PSNI in 2012 ‘Regional Guidance Police Intervention in Residential Units.  Safeguarding of Children Missing from Home and Foster Care May 2012’ with updated versions in 2015 ‘Missing Children Protocol (Runaway and Missing from Care and Home)’, and in 2023 ‘Interface Protocol between HSC Trusts and PSNI (Where a child is reported missing and other police interactions with residential children’s homes)’.how many residential care homes have used it It is the responsibility of the individual HSC Trust to ensure the reporting of all incidents to the Department of Health (SPPG) directly for Looked After Children for whom they are responsible, regardless of where the child is placed i.e. in a Children’s Home, Foster Care, supporting living complex, living at home etc. Where a young person has a placement in a regional facility such as Lakewood Secure Care Centre, the proforma should be submitted from the Trust who placed the child there. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider legislation to allow local planning authorities the power to remove advertising which is in breach of planning regulations, similar to the powers granted to DfI Roads.
answer_text: While there is currently no corresponding provision within The Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (the 2011 Act) or The Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (the Advertisement Regulations) similar to the Roads (NI) Order 1993, there are other statutory regimes providing legislative provisions to enable councils to remove certain types of advertisements in their district.  Part 4 of the Clean Neighbourhoods and Environment Act (NI) 2011 enables councils to serve a ‘defacement removal notice’ for certain types of advertisements, namely graffiti, posters or placards which either, contravene section 130 of the 2011 Act, are offensive, or are detrimental to the amenity of the district.  The notice sets out the requirement to remove, clear or remedy the particular defacement within a period no less than 28 days.  Where the notice is not complied with, the council may remove, clear or remedy the defacement, and may seek to recover incurred expenses.Councils may, of course, continue to exercise their powers under regulation 7 of the Advertisement Regulations which enables a council to remedy what appears to be a substantial injury to the amenity of the locality or a danger to members of the public, by serving a discontinuance notice on the person displaying the advertisement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether DfI Rivers’ Planning Advisory and Modelling Unit has the appropriate competence and training to respond to requests from local planning authorities in relation to flood mitigation measures.
answer_text: My Department’s Planning Advisory Unit is a Statutory Consultee to local Planning Authorities in relation to planning and flood risk. Planning Advisory Unit staff are trained and competent in providing advice to requests from local Planning Authorities in relation to flood mitigation measures offered for river and coastal flood risk.  In addition my Department’s Planning Advisory Unit staff are also trained and competent in providing advice on mitigation measures in relation to surface water flood risk outside river and coastal flood plains, with the exception of those drainage systems that involve “soft SuDS” proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider legislation to amend section 132 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 to extend the enforcement period for (i) changes of use; and (ii) conditions, from five years to ten years, in line with other jurisdictions in the UK.
answer_text: The single 5-year time period for all breaches of planning control was introduced, under the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, to create a more efficient system of control, less confusion and greater clarity in dealing with breaches of planning control. This change came as a result of a proposed amendment to the Planning Bill by the then Environment Committee, which was accepted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether DfI Rivers’ Planning Advisory and Modelling Unit has the appropriate competence and training to respond to requests from local planning authorities in relation to the efficacy of proposed Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS).
answer_text: My Department’s Planning Advisory Unit is a Statutory Consultee to local Planning Authorities in relation to planning and flood risk. Planning Advisory Unit staff are trained and competent in providing advice on those Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS) that are adoptable under the Water and Sewerage Services Act (NI) 2016 and generally referred to as ‘hard SuDS’. They are normally large storage pipes or tanks, usually with a flow control valve, and will be constructed to appropriate standards and maintained by NI Water.  However, Planning Advisory Unit do not have the knowledge and expertise to comment on the efficacy of ‘soft SuDS’, such as ponds or swales, nor do they have legislative remit to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what plans his Department has to address coastal erosion in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: Coastal Change Risk Management is not the responsibility of any department in Northern Ireland.  This legislative gap was recognised by previous Ministers, and I will be discussing this issue with the Infrastructure Minister and Executive colleagues. Under the existing policy and legislative framework, my Department has no role in addressing coastal erosion.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will support a future bid for stages of the World Rally Championship to be held in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Major events are currently considered on a case-by-case basis and are subject to additional funding being made available. For new major events, such as the World Rally Championship, affordability is a key consideration and will therefore need to be considered by the Executive as a whole to ensure that they meet the Executive’s overall objectives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the length of time taken by councils to implement local development plans.
answer_text: Multiple factors have contributed to the time taken in bringing forward Local Development Plans as we move towards bringing a full cycle of Plans through the system. As each document goes through the process everyone’s understanding evolves and consideration is being given to best practice and lessons learnt, particularly for those councils that remain to publish their Plan Strategies.You will be aware last year in 2023, my Department directed 4 councils to adopt their Plan Strategies with modifications, reflecting the recent progress that is being made.   The Plan process is also reliant on a robust evidence base to develop sound policies, so every council must take time to gather and consider their evidence carefully.  My officials encourage “front loading” the system; whereby councils can share their policy approach with the statutory consultees for comment prior to publication of their Plans. Many councils have taken up this offer of assistance and it has proven to be beneficial, reducing the issues to be considered at independent Examination. However, I am determined that we need to build further upon the progress, and through the Planning improvement Programme stakeholders including my Department, Councils and statutory consultees are reviewing the current processes so the plan system can be more responsive in our drive to support sustainable development for our businesses and communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what low carbon schemes are being provided by the Housing Executive in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: Low carbon schemes being provided by the Housing Executive in the Strangford constituency are operational matters for the Housing Executive. You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the criteria for taking part in the heat pump schemes being delivered by the Housing Executive.
answer_text: The criteria for taking part in the heat pump schemes being delivered by the Housing Executive is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what steps his Department will take to encourage and incentivise more businesses into commercial premises in town centres in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: Sub-regional economic development, including addressing regional imbalances, is one of my core priorities.  I will work with a range of local partners including local enterprise agencies, local businesses, the community and voluntary sector, Invest NI and councils, who have a lead role in progressing and promoting local economic development policies to improve town centres.  I also intend to work with Executive colleagues on taking forward the High Street Task Force recommendations to benefit town centres. This will necessarily require agreement from other Ministers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 5529/22-27, when the consultation on the Northern Ireland Waste Management Strategy will be launched.
answer_text: My officials are finalising the draft Waste Management Strategy. This will be consulted on later in 2024, following the upcoming consultation on recycling. 		This approach has been taken following stakeholder feedback, as many in the sector will respond to both consultations. These are lengthy and complex documents and my Department recognises the benefit in staggering the launch to allow stakeholders the time necessary to provide valuable and meaningful input.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what measures are taken to ensure pupils being home schooled, whether electively or not, receive a full-time education suitable to their age, ability and aptitude and to any special educational needs the child may have.
answer_text: Parents are responsible for ensuring that their child receives an education, either through regular attendance at school or otherwise. The EA has published guidelines to support parents who choose to educate their children outside of the formal school system which provide information on home education provision and outline the roles and responsibilities of parents in ensuring their child receives a suitable education. Information is included on special educational needs and on organisations that support home educating families.  Where a concern is raised about the education provided for a home educated child, the EA has a duty to make enquiries and parents must satisfy the EA that their child is receiving a suitable education. The process is detailed in the guidelines: https://www.eani.org.uk/services/elective-home-education.Elective home education is different to tuition which may be provided by the EA to children in their own homes in certain exceptional circumstances. The EA Exceptional Teaching Arrangements Service provides short term support to children unable to attend school to help them to maintain educational engagement in core subjects and re-integrate into school.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many social housing applicants with a disability have been identified as in need of accessible housing in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: The number of applicants on the social housing waiting list with a disability and identified as being in need of accessible housing in the Strangford constituency is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps are being taken to progress the six recommendations made following the review of restraint and seclusion in educational settings.
answer_text: In terms of recommendation 1, which relates to the repeal of Article 4 1 (c) of the 1998 Education Order,  I plan to consider this matter further with my officials before determining the appropriate next steps. Recommendations 2, 3 and 5 relate to the publication of statutory guidance on the use of restrictive and supportive practices in educational settings. Department of Education officials engaged with a wide range of stakeholders to co-design draft statutory guidance in accordance with the report recommendations, and a public consultation on the draft guidance ran from 5th September to 10th November 2023. The responses to the consultation are currently being analysed.  Subject to my approval, the consultation report will be published on the Departmental website in due course and I will consider the next steps to be taken thereafter.On recommendation 4, officials continue to work with health colleagues to develop regional guidance on pupils’ medication and healthcare needs including the use of equipment in educational settings. It is anticipated that guidance in relation to medication needs will be ready for publication during summer term.  Recommendation 6, which relates to the Department commissioning a periodic review and publishing a report on the use of restrictive and supportive practices in educational settings, can only be progressed once the statutory guidance is in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 6205/22-27, to detail the number of pupils (i) attending independent schools; and (ii) being electively home educated, in each of the preceding five years.
answer_text: The table below provides the number of pupils attending independent schools in each of the preceding five years.Academic YearEnrolment2022/237912021/227162020/216252019/206082018/19611Source: Annual School Census(ii) The Education Authority (EA) has provided the information below in respect of the number of children it was informed of who were being electively home educated in each of the preceding five years.  These figures do not reflect the entirety of children in Northern Ireland who are electively home educated.  Academic YearNumber of children recorded by EA as being electively home educated2022/239072021/228082020/217102019/204722018/19439Parents are responsible for ensuring their child receives an education, at school or otherwise. Parents may therefore choose to educate their children at home rather than register them at a school and they may choose to deregister their child from school with the intention of educating them at home. Whilst the EA is aware of children who have attended and subsequently been deregistered from a school, there is no duty on parents to inform the Department of Education or the EA if they have chosen to educate their children at home. Thus, there is no complete record of children being electively home educated in Northern Ireland. This is the case in other jurisdictions.

answer_text: My Department does not maintain records of funding in the format requested, however details of expenditure on Structural Maintenance outturns analysed between resource and capital can be provided.Budget is allocated to Roads Divisions, as opposed to departmental offices. Expenditure on Structural Maintenance is detailed in the Table 1 below for each of the last five financial years. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will be releasing a further round of funding for the Small Settlements Regeneration Programme.
answer_text: This question has been transferred to the Department for Communities as my Department is delivering the Covid Recovery Small Settlements Regeneration Programme.The Covid Recovery Small Settlements Regeneration Programme is a collaborative project between my Department, the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs and the Department for Infrastructure. Its aim is to support rural communities and smaller settlements to recover from the impact of the pandemic. The programme will see a total investment of over £28m into small settlements, supporting regeneration projects designed to make small towns and villages more appealing, accessible and vibrant. Projects are due to complete by the end of March 2025. There are currently no plans for a further Covid Recovery Small Settlements Regeneration Programme as the scheme was designed as a specific response to the pandemic.Once the programme has been concluded and evaluated my Department will consider any lessons learnt and the need for future similar initiatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any plans to dispose of the site and buildings of the old Ballykeigle Primary School, Comber.
answer_text: The former Ballykeigle Primary School site was disposed of by the Education Authority in June 2021.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) what budget the Office of Environmental Protection requires to carry out its functions; and (ii) whether it has been fully financed.
answer_text: DAERA and the Defra Secretary of State have a legal duty to fund the Office for Environmental Protection (OEP) at a level they consider is reasonably sufficient to enable the OEP to carry out its functions. However, as a new and unique organisation, it will take time to determine the appropriate level of funding. In its first full year of operation in NI, 2022-23, the OEP sought a budget of £856k.  It received £670k resource funding from my Department but was unable to spend its full allocation and returned £71k.  In 2023-24, my Department provided an increased resource allocation of £1,250k.   In common with almost all public bodies here, the OEP did not receive all that it asked for in what was an extremely difficult public expenditure context.Allocations for 2024-25 will depend on available budgets.  My officials are working closely with the OEP and with their counterparts in Defra to develop a sustainable long-term funding model for the OEP.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will be releasing new rural development funding.
answer_text: Although the NI Rural Development Programme has ended, the Department continues to deliver funding to rural communities through a range of NI Executive and Departmental Interventions including Rural Policy Pilots, the Tackling Rural Poverty and Social Isolation (TRPSI) programme, PEACEPLUS Programme, City & Growth Deals, the Complementary Fund and the Small Settlements Regeneration Programme. 		As a priority I will be considering the future of rural policy, including how rural communities can be supported following the ending of the NI Rural Development Programme.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how many people are on the social housing waiting list in the Strangford constituency; and (ii) how many on the list are considered homeless.
answer_text: The number of people on the social housing waiting list and how many are considered homeless in the Strangford constituency is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.You should, therefore, direct your question to the Housing Executive at its dedicated MLA response team, which can be contacted at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the timescale for the installation of lighting along the Comber Greenway from Dundonald to Comber; and (ii) whether consideration is being given to grit this section of the greenway.
answer_text: A lighting scheme was installed as a pilot in April 2023 and is currently in operation between the Beersbridge Road and the Billy Neill Soccer Centre of Excellence on the Comber Greenway.   A consultation will be taken forward after a two year trial period to provide an opportunity for comments on this project that will inform a decision on whether the scheme should be extended along the Greenway or to other locations. While there is no statutory requirement to grit roads or footpaths, my Department delivers a winter service programme to maintain road safety and to sustain the economic movement of vehicular traffic during wintery conditions. To extend this service to the Comber Greenway or other greenways would present a significant logistical, financial and resource challenge which, with so many other competing safety related pressures, my Department would be unable to take forward at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his plans for social and affordable housing in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: As NIHE have statutory responsibility for assessing social and intermediate housing need in specific locations this is an operational matter for NIHE. You should, therefore, direct your question to the NIHE at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk. Local Councils will consider their own affordable housing needs for their areas, in conjunction with NIHE, through the development and delivery of Local Development Plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the schools in the Strangford constituency to be included in the 20mph speed limit schemes.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints.  Schools have been assessed in accordance with DfI’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructureni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070 The program to date has been rolled out in tranches, with the most recent completed in November 2022.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on plans to increase the provision of NHS dentists in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: A key part of the access issues facing patients are the rates being paid to our dentists for treatment.  The absence of Executive Ministers has meant that it has not been possible to progress the recommended pay uplifts for staffing groups for 2023/24.  This is now my immediate priority, and I am meeting with Trade Unions in early course to take this forward. Officials have already been making arrangements in anticipation of funding being made available to ensure that uplifts can be implemented as quickly as possible.I know that an additional £95m has been invested in General Dental Services via the Financial Support Scheme and Rebuilding Support Scheme since April 2020. This came to an end in July 2023 due to the severe financial pressures arising from the Department’s 2023/24 budget settlement.A pilot scheme is currently running to provide appointments to unregistered patients who require emergency dental treatment during the working week. If a patient is not registered with a dentist, requires emergency dental treatment, and has been unable to access an appointment, they can contact their local Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) office for advice.I am also conscious of the progress that has been made in the rest of the UK on dental reform and I will be discussing these issues with officials as soon as possible, to ensure that the sector here in NI is supported in delivering important care to patients. More immediately, officials have been developing investment proposals to further support General Dental Services and I will consider them in early course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how much Invest NI has spent in the Strangford constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with 2022-23, ending 31st March 2023, being the most recent year available.  Information relating to 2023-24 is provisionally planned for release in June 2024.  The table below details the amount of financial assistance that Invest NI has offered towards projects in the Strangford Parliamentary Constituency between 2018-19 and 2022-23.In addition to financial assistance, Invest NI also provides wider support to all businesses through advice, assistance or guidance from our staff and through nibusinessinfo.co.uk, our online business advice portal.Invest NI Financial Assistance Offered in Strangford Parliamentary Constituency (2018-19 to 2022-23)YearTotal Invest NI Assistance £m2018-191.762019-201.592020-213.652021-221.362022-231.60Total9.96

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) whether there are any proposals for public art projects; (ii) where these projects are; and (iii) what budget is available for each of these projects.
answer_text: There are currently six proposals for public art projects to be delivered by my Department or by Councils with funding contributions from my Department. The projects are in Londonderry, Larne, Ballymena, Bangor and Belfast.There are two projects in Londonderry with a commitment of £187,000 to one and £97,500 to the other. For the projects in Larne and Ballymena each has received a commitment of £27,000. The proposals in Bangor and Belfast are still under consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much has been spent on (i) pothole repairs; and (ii) resurfacing schemes in the Strangford constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department does not maintain records of expenditure in the format requested, however details of total Structural Maintenance expenditure (resource and capital) can be provided for the two councils which Strangford Constituency is part of.Table 1: Total Structural Maintenance£’mTotal Structural Maintenance 18-1919-20 20-21 21-2222-23Ards & North Down Borough Council5.34.65.05.93.0Newry, Mourne and Down District Council12.613.711.89.419.5Structural Maintenance covers patching of carriageway and footways, resurfacing and reconstruction, asphalt patching, surface dressing, capital investment in both drainage infrastructure and in the structural stability of embankments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how much his Department has spent on public art in each of the last five years; and (ii) to detail the projects on which this has been spent.
answer_text: Financial YearSpend (£)Name of Project2018-192,400Cathedral Corridor Hill Street wall mural, Newry2019-2002020-2172,000Traditional Jaunting Car and Driver sculpture, Pound Street, Larne2021-2258,500Five Cs murals, Belfast98,000Frederick Douglass statue, Belfast11,500Art Jam, Newtownards3,962Church Street wall mural, Downpatrick5,000Mosaic Artwork between Town Hall and Pound Street, Larne2,995Photograph installation at Main Street, Larne10,994Harbour Square Public Realm, Londonderry85,48023 street art installations across six towns in Causeway Coast & Glens Borough Council area2022-2313,000“Guess How much I Love You” themed artwork at Haslem Lane, Lisburn2,995Church Street Arcade wall mural, Ballymena46,386.20Five feature street art murals in Ballymena & Larne10,000Harbour Square Public Realm, Londonderry37,39930 art installations in Coleraine town centre including ‘The Hands of Compassion’ in New Row Total460,611.20

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will carry out a review of the independence of the Drainage Council for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The existing Memorandum of Understanding between my Department and the Drainage Council sets out a requirement for the Department to undertake an effectiveness review to help support governance arrangements and complement the risk management and assessment process. My officials are proceeding to progress this piece of work, in line with the existing annual appraisal mechanism.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 5530/22-27, whether potato assessors completed onsite appraisals of the damage caused by floods last autumn.
answer_text: Following the flooding in November 2023, the farms of several potato growers were visited to see the damage caused and to provide technical advice on drying, storage and handling of crops. DAERA officials also gathered information through direct feedback from other growers and discussions at a Potato Sector Business Development Group (BDG) meeting in the region.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how many claims have been submitted for damage to vehicles as a result of poor road surfaces in the Strangford constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The management information my Department holds in relation to roads related public liability claims can be found online at:Road Asset Maintenance Directorate - Management Information on roads related claims for the period 2018/2019 to 2022/2023 | Department for Infrastructure (infrastructure-ni.gov.uk)Table 3 in the above link provides details of vehicle damage claims received during the last five full financial years due to road defects and other issues on the public road network.  However, please note that my Department maintains its records by Roads Section Office, which broadly align with Council areas.  The Strangford constituency is covered by the Ards and North Down, Lisburn and Castlereagh and Newry, Mourne and Down Roads Section Offices.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the training provided to members of the Drainage Council for Northern Ireland prior to, and during, their tenure; and (ii) who provides this training.
answer_text: Members of the Drainage Council receive an induction by my Officials prior to commencing their role, providing them with an overview of the work carried out by my Department’s Rivers Directorate.The Drainage Council consists of members from a very diverse range of specialisms each bringing a wealth of knowledge from their area of expertise i.e. fisheries, agriculture, tourism, conservation. Officials met with the Chair of the Drainage Council in January 2023 to consider the training requirements of Council members. A list of learning requirements was developed and shared with Council members in February 2023.  However, given the diverse range of Council members, officials concluded that the optimal learning method would be to develop members learning as they fulfilled their duties and this approach was subsequently conveyed to members.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the lease for Ballynahinch Olympic Football Club to use the Ballynahinch High School pitch.
answer_text: The Education Authority has offered to enter into a license agreement with the Ballynahinch Olympic Football Club, however, the Club have requested that a lease be granted.The EA has agreed to undertake a review of this case. This review will be completed shortly and a response to Ballynahinch Olympic Football Club will be provided as soon as possible thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how much was expended on the COVID Status Certification in Domestic Settings scheme.
answer_text: The COVID Certification Service (CCS) was implemented to support citizens wishing to undertake international travel to evidence their COVID status (to EU/ WHO specification) to meet the requirements of destination countries. The CCS, and supporting infrastructure, was continuously updated during its lifetime as new requirements arose, including to assist the holder of a certificate to meet UK regional requirements for entry into various events and venues across the UK and Ireland. It is not possible to disaggregate the cost of the domestic certification functionality from that required for international travel.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what efforts have been undertaken to source funding to restore the Martin Memorial Clock in Shrigley, County Down.
answer_text: Sourcing funding to restore the Martin Memorial Clock in Shrigley, County Down, is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. 		You should therefore direct your question to the Northern Ireland Housing Executive at public.affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the upgrade to expand the capacity of the sewerage and storm drains in Wicket Lane, Saintfield.
answer_text: The issue you raised is an operational matter for NI Water.  You should therefore, direct your question to NI Water, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when Ballynahinch Primary School will be included in the the 20mph speed limit scheme.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints.  Schools have been assessed in accordance with DfI’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070 The program to date has been rolled out in tranches, with the most recent completed in November 2022.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how much compensation has been paid by each Health and Social Care Trust for medical negligence claims, in each of the last two years.
answer_text: Information on how much compensation has been paid by each Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust on clinical / social care negligence cases each year, and compared with each of the previous four years, is published annually in Table 1 of the “Clinical / Social Care Negligence Cases in Northern Ireland” report, the latest of which is available to view or download from:Clinical / Social Care Negligence Cases in Northern Ireland 2022/23 (health-ni.gov.uk)

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of pupils (i) attending independent schools; and (ii) being home schooled.
answer_text: There are currently 772 pupils enrolled in independent schools.The Education Authority (EA) has advised that it has been informed of 861 children that are currently being electively home educated, however it is important to note that this figure does not reflect the entirety of children in Northern Ireland who are electively home educated. Parents are responsible for ensuring their child receives an education, at school or otherwise. Parents may therefore choose to educate their children at home rather than register them at a school and they may choose to deregister their child from school with the intention of educating them at home. Whilst the EA is aware of children who have attended and subsequently been deregistered from a school, there is no duty on parents to inform the Department of Education or the EA if they have chosen to educate their children at home. Thus, there is no complete record of children being electively home educated in Northern Ireland. This is the case in other jurisdictions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration has been given to moving the 30mph speed limit on Killinchy Road, Comber, to the town’s settlement limit following recent housing development.
answer_text: When considering the appropriate speed limit for a road the Department’s policy and procedures guide entitled “Setting Local Speed Limits in Northern Ireland” is used to ensure speed limits are consistent and motorists are presented with the same speed limit on similar roads taking into account factors such as: the characteristic of the road; the speed of existing traffic; the degree of frontage development; and road traffic collision data, which is continuously monitored using data provided by the PSNI.The current speed limits on the A22 Killinchy Road are in line with our current policy document and considered to be appropriate.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding, or parking which causes an obstruction to other road (and footway) users should be directed to the police using the non-emergency number 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the effectiveness of the Healthy Happy Minds scheme.
answer_text: The Healthy Happy Minds Pilot concluded on 31 March 2023.  An evaluation of the pilot has been carried out and I will assess its findings as I seek to better support the emotional health and wellbeing of learners.  The Independent Counselling Service for Schools (ICSS) remains available to post-primary age pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what the average waiting time is for a non-urgent appointment with an NHS dentist in (i) Northern Ireland; and (ii) the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: This information is not recorded by NI General Dental Services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many NHS dentists are registered in the Strangford constituency.
answer_text: The headcount number of dentists, who have made arrangements with the Strategic Planning and Performance Group to provide health service dentistry in the Strangford Assembly Area at 31st December 2023 was 50, using data from the latest publicly available quarterly Accredited Official Statistics.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what plans there are for the expansion of accommodation and facilities at Oakwood School and Assessment Centre, Saintfield.
answer_text: A business case has been approved for a double modular classroom at Oakwood School and Assessment Centre.  It is currently anticipated that this will be delivered in September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what funding is available for local councils to provide CCTV in towns.
answer_text: My Department does not specifically fund town centre or public-facing CCTV cameras in any council area and has no funding streams available for CCTV related projects.The Interfaces Programme does occasionally receive requests to contribute to the costs of upgrading CCTV cameras specifically linked to interface work. Such requests are considered on a case-by-case basis and, if approved, the Department is resourced through the Executive T:BUC Strategy funding allocation to the Interfaces Programme to facilitate the work required; however, these are very peripheral engagements in the overall context of the provision of CCTV across Northern Ireland.My Department, together with the Northern Ireland Policing Board, provides substantial annual funding to Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs) to lead on the Department’s response to community safety issues within each council area.  PCSPs may contribute towards capital CCTV costs as part of a cocktail of funding, if the PCSP agrees that funding should be utilised for this purpose.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether (i) any alternative sites for a new library in Newtownards are being considered; and (ii) a timeline has been established for the delivery of a new library in Newtownards.
answer_text: Libraries NI has submitted a business case to my Department proposing the redevelopment of Newtownards library. In preparing this business case, Libraries NI considered a range of potential location and scale options.  The preferred option put forward is for a partnership project with Ards and North Down Borough Council to redevelop the library on its present site.   No definitive timeline has been set for the delivery of the project. Any decision to proceed with a new library facility will be dependent on prioritising of capital investment in a very tight budgetary situation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what Equality Impact Assessment was carried out in connection with the closure of the Newtownards and Bangor Minor Injuries Units.
answer_text: A Equality Impact Assessment was carried by South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust as part of the consultation exercise.  “The Future Provision of Trust Urgent and emergency Care Services – Ards and North Down Area Equality Impact Assessment” can be found on the Trust website at Consultations - South Eastern Health & Social Care Trust (hscni.net).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will reconsider the points allocation system for social housing in respect of intimidation points.
answer_text: Work has been ongoing in relation to the Fundamental Review of Social Housing Allocations. My officials are working with the Housing Executive to consider the way forward for the proposal relating to intimidation points. This includes independent research which is due to complete by the end of March 2024.Once options in relation to this proposal have been further developed, I will give them my consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will make an announcement on the Sub Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: I recognise and share the deep frustration of many that this programme has not been delivered. Work is progressing to develop proposals for the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Football and I have asked my officials to present me with proposals and options for consideration as a matter of priority. Once final proposals are confirmed and approvals are secured, I will be able to provide a timeframe for the process to apply for funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the (i) Ballynahinch bypass; and (ii) Comber bypass.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times, increase reliability and improve road safety.The A24 Ballynahinch Bypass will deliver a 3.1km bypass to the east which will improve traffic conditions in the town centre. Details of the current status of the A24 Ballynahinch Bypass project will continue to be available on the project webpage which can be viewed at: A24 Ballynahinch Bypass - overview | Department for Infrastructure (infrastructure-ni.gov.uk)Comber Bypass is not a current scheme listed on the Major Works Programme. Any decision on an additional phase of the Comber Bypass will be informed through the development of the Eastern Transport Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what steps will be taken to address the findings by the Criminal Justice Inspectorate in relation to drug use at HMP Magilligan since its 2021 inspection.
answer_text: The recent Independent Review of Progress at Magilligan Prison noted that a revised Drug and Alcohol Strategy had been introduced in September 2023. This Strategy is now embedded and an Action Plan designed to restrict supply, reduce demand and build recovery is in place.Key to this are partnerships with North West Regional College, South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust, AD:EPT (Alcohol and Drugs: Empowering People through Therapy) and other stakeholders. This integrated approach has been designed to boost purposeful activity, maintain family links and empower individuals’ self-esteem to break the cycle of substance misuse. Positive results have already been evidenced from these interventions.The Northern Ireland Prison Service (NIPS) continues to work closely with the Police Service of Northern Ireland to tackle the flow of drugs into prisons. In addition, on a daily basis NIPS deploys X-ray body scanner technology and other search aids to thwart efforts to traffic prohibited items into establishments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail the amount of funding being allocated to the (a) Comber park and ride project; and (b) Newtownards park and ride project; and (ii) for an update on the progress for both sites.
answer_text: A suitable site for Comber Park & Ride facility has been identified along the A22 Belfast Road corridor immediately adjacent to the Comber Greenway with the potential to provide parking provision for 626 vehicles.  The current cost estimate for the scheme is £4.1m.  Officials in my department are progressing the relevant documentation in advance of a formal submission of a planning application.A suitable site for a Newtownards Park & Ride facility has been identified at the former Newtownards Leisure Centre adjacent to the Hardford Link and William Street with the potential to provide parking provision for 430 vehicles.  My Department has approved funding of £250,000 to Translink to progress the development of the project on behalf of my Department.  A planning application for the development of the site was submitted in September 2023.  A business case to secure the necessary funding, currently in the region of £7m, is being prepared.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current staffing structure of Sport NI, including (i) the title of all posts where staff have been appointed on a temporary promotion basis; (ii) when those appointments were made; and (iii) when it is anticipated that recruitment will take place to fill those posts on a permanent basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much funding has been provided to the Northern Cricket Union in the each of the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has provided the following funding to the Northern Cricket Union in the last five years:Fiscal YearTypeProgrammeAmount2019/20CapitalPractice wicket£30,0002019/20RevenueCricket development£42,500In addition, Sport NI the Department’s Arm’s Length Body provided the Northern Cricket Union with £15,217 of Exchequer revenue funding through the Covid Recovery Programme in 2021.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much funding has been provided to Cricket Ireland in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The information requested on how much funding has been provided to Cricket Ireland in the each of last five years is an operational matter for Sport NI.You should, therefore, direct your question to Richard Archibald, Interim Chief Executive Sport NI, at Assemblybusiness@sportni.net.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will publish an updated Development Control Advice Note 10 for (i) The Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015; and (ii) The Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2017 and associated case law.
answer_text: As part of my Department’s Environmental Governance Work Programme (EGWP) work has been taken forward over recent years with district councils, in their role as local planning authorities (LPAs), to enhance understanding and capacity in relation to good environmental practice, with a particular focus on environmental impact assessment (EIA).  This EGWP work has included the development of a number of development management practice notes (DMPNs) in key areas of the EIA process prioritised in discussion with LPAs.DMPN 9A on ‘Unauthorised EIA Development’ was published in December 2021.  DMPN 9B on ‘Screening Projects for EIA’ was published in December 2023.  Further work is continuing on DMPN 9C on ‘Scoping EIA Development’, and it is intended to publish this practice note in the near futureThese practice notes are, in effect, an update to and replacement for DCAN 10 and are intended to assist planning authorities in promoting consistency in approach in applying the statutory requirements of the Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2017, which are the most up to date and relevant EIA Regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will update the Annex B map of protected routes attached to PPS3: Access, Movement and Parking.
answer_text: The Clarification of Policy AMP 3: Access to Protected Routes, to Planning Policy Statement 3 (PPS 3) was published in October 2006.  At the time of the publication a map identified the existing roads throughout the North established as Protected Routes (Annex A).The protected routes map, which has been available on the Departmental website since 2018, is being updated and is to be made available by the start of April. See following link:https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/development-control-information-developers

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much funding has been provided to Northern Cricket Union affiliated clubs in the each of last five years.
answer_text: The information requested on how much funding has been provided to Northern Cricket Union affiliated clubs in the each of last five yearsis an operational matter for Sport NI.You should, therefore, direct your question to Richard Archibald, Interim Chief Executive Sport NI, at Assemblybusiness@sportni.net.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what measures his Department is taking to improve road safety on Portaferry Road, Newtownards.
answer_text: I am advised that a number of road safety measures have been installed at this location including upgrading the warning signage, verge marker posts, and white lines.  However, I have asked my officials to review the existing road markings and marker posts to establish if they need refreshed again to help with driver visibility, as well as considering the need for future resurfacing works.My department is currently reviewing a stretch of the A20 Portaferry Road from Newtownards to Greyabbey, in particular to consider any collisions that have occurred along this stretch, and their causation factors.  This review will be used to determine if any additional safety measures could be introduced to improve road safety. Any resulting improvements will be included in future annual Roads Reports to Council.I am also advised that the current speed limit on the A20 Portaferry Road is 60mph (national speed limit) and is in line with the Department’s current policy document RSPPG E051 (Setting local speed limits in Northern Ireland).Issues relating to poor driver behaviour should be directed to the PSNI who has the responsibility for enforcement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how much has been spent to date on the Casement Park project by (i) the Department; and (ii) the GAA.
answer_text: Work is progressing on the Casement Park project, including the completion of an updated Business Case.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration his Department has given to reducing the speed limit on Portaferry Road at Teal Rocks, Newtownards, following the development of an additional 100 homes.
answer_text: When considering the appropriate speed limit for a road the Department’s policy and procedures guide entitled “Setting Local Speed Limits in Northern Ireland” is used to ensure speed limits are consistent and motorists are presented with the same speed limit on similar roads taking into account factors such as: the characteristic of the road; the speed of existing traffic; the degree of frontage development; and road traffic collision data, which is continuously monitored using data provided by the PSNI.The current speed limit on the A20 Portaferry Road is 60mph (national speed limit) is in line with our current policy document and considered to be appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether there has been an updated business case developed for the Casement Park project.
answer_text: Work is progressing on the Casement Park project, including the completion of an updated Business Case.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether further consideration will be given to a graduated reduction in speed on the A21 Ballygowan Road approaching Comber.
answer_text: When considering the appropriate speed limit for a road the Department’s policy and procedures guide entitled “Setting Local Speed Limits in Northern Ireland” is used to ensure speed limits are consistent and motorists are presented with the same speed limit on similar roads taking into account factors such as: the characteristic of the road; the speed of existing traffic; the degree of frontage development; and road traffic collision data, which is continuously monitored using data provided by the PSNI.I am advised that the current speed limit is consistent with the nature of the road and is appropriate at this location. As such there are no plans to introduce a gradual reduction of the speed limit on the A21 Ballygowan Road approaching Comber.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether consideration will be given to moving the 30mph speed limit on the Comber Road, Ballygowan, to include Olivet Court and the Platinum Jubilee Park.
answer_text: When considering the appropriate speed limit for a road the Department’s policy and procedures guide entitled “Setting Local Speed Limits in Northern Ireland” is used to ensure speed limits are consistent and motorists are presented with the same speed limit on similar roads taking into account factors such as: the characteristic of the road; the speed of existing traffic; the degree of frontage development; and road traffic collision data, which is continuously monitored using data provided by the PSNI.I am advised that the current speed limit is consistent with the nature of the road and is appropriate at this location and as such there are no plans to extend the 30mph speed limit to Olivet Court and Platinum Park.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the support packages in place for potato and vegetable farms impacted by floods last autumn.
answer_text: A business case was completed by DAERA to examine and test rigorously the need and value for money of a financial support scheme for potato growers impacted by flooding in counties Armagh and Down. The conclusion was that government intervention is unlikely to confer a net benefit to the NI economy that would justify expenditure. For this reason, DAERA has not implemented a support scheme. I remain open to considering further representations on this matter from the sector.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs on which date the Waste Strategy for Northern Ireland will be published.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Resources & Waste Management Strategy is currently under development, with a view to launching a consultation this year. A date for publication cannot be set until a consultation has taken place and responses have been analysed.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what funding will be made available to local councils to implement any recommendations contained in the Waste Strategy for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Resources & Waste Management Strategy is currently under development, with a view to consulting this year. The consultation will seek views on the commitments, targets, policies, and proposals that will shape the direction of waste management for the next six years as well as fulfilling the legal obligations and ambitions of the Department given the significant legislative changes since the last strategy.		Following consultation, I will be able to assess which recommendations are to be implemented and their financial implications. Impact assessments will identify what funding is required to implement the strategy and new schemes may need to be developed to fund these.		Currently, the Household Waste Recycling Collaborative Change Programme provides capital funding assistance for local councils to improve recycling infrastructure and services. Going forward, projects will be supported with grants of up to 50% from DAERA.		Officials have now written to all council chief executives and directors of environmental services advising them of the revised HWRCCP criteria and arrangements for submitting applications.  Further information on the fund and application process can be found on the DAERA website.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
